case_ch:
In May 2026, Professor Wang traveled from Hangzhou to Beijing to attend a project seminar. The round-trip itinerary was charged to research project funds, and after returning to campus, he organized the reimbursement documents for both this trip and the supplies purchased afterward.
For the travel materials, the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" had been completed, signed by the unit head, and affixed with the official seal of the department (the first-level budget unit). This was not a trip taken by the unit head personally. The itinerary was calculated as 3 calendar days, with 2 nights of lodging in Beijing. The lodging standard was filled in according to the standard for bureau-director-level personnel and persons of equivalent rank, and the hotel rate was RMB 600 per night. For intercity transportation, he traveled by train in first-class train seating. Both the intercity transportation invoice and the lodging invoice were attached. There was no special explanation for situations such as being unable to obtain transportation receipts in a remote area or being unable to obtain a lodging invoice, and there was also no case in which an external unit shared the intercity transportation, lodging, meal subsidy, or city transportation expenses. A meal subsidy of RMB 300 was claimed. City transportation expenses of RMB 180 were reimbursed against receipts on an actual basis, not as a lump-sum allowance. There was no self-driving or car rental.
After the seminar, Professor Wang also purchased some routine office supplies for organizing materials, including office stationery, newspapers, and magazines. The amount did not reach the fixed asset threshold and did not fall within the review scope for externally purchased inventory by the Asset Management Office. The seller provided a goods list together with the invoice. There was no online shopping transaction record involved, nor did the case involve books and materials, printing and production, page charges, or monograph publication subsidies.

case_ch:
In April 2026, the university hosted a national teaching forum, and all related expenses were processed together after the event. The forum notice, agenda, budget form, detailed list of conference expense expenditures, sign-in sheet of actual attendees, service invoices, original supporting documents, electronic settlement document, and entrustment agreement (contract) were all submitted together.
This event was charged to research project funds. There were about 100 participants, and the conference lasted 2 days. The conference services uniformly arranged lodging and meals. The lodging, meal, and other expenses were reported at RMB 500, RMB 150, and RMB 110 per person per day respectively, while the total was written as RMB 760 per person per day. No prohibited items were included in the budget, there was no advance payment before the event, and it was not a case where more than 50% of the participating units were based in Shanghai but the meeting was held outside Shanghai. The entire expense was reimbursed in a single batch through the same channel.
Before the forum, a print shop was also entrusted to prepare conference supplies, and some office stationery was purchased for binding the conference handbook. The seller provided a goods list, but in order to avoid reaching the threshold for a required review list, the invoice amounts were artificially split across invoices issued during the same period.

case_ch:
In May 2026, Professor Li's unit organized a "University Financial Digitalization Training Program," and after the training ended, the training expenses were sorted out together. During the same period, Professor Li himself also traveled to Beijing to handle project matters, and the related travel receipts were submitted together with the funding materials.
For the training portion, the expenses were reported under the standard applicable to bureau-director-level personnel. The training lasted 15 days, and the total of lodging, meal, venue and materials expenses, and other expenses was RMB 13,200, including RMB 7,500 for lodging, RMB 2,250 for meals, RMB 1,200 for venue and materials expenses, and RMB 450 for other expenses. During the training period, an instructor was invited from another city, and the total lecture fees were RMB 1,800. The instructor's lodging, meal, and intercity transportation expenses were reimbursed at RMB 500, RMB 150, and RMB 400 respectively. The list of trainees and their contact information, along with the invoices provided by the training service provider, had all been prepared. A separate written approval was also attached for the portion involving an increased standard. However, the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures" was not submitted with the reimbursement package.
Professor Li's own trip to Beijing was charged to research project funds. The "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" was attached for reimbursement, and because this was a trip taken by the unit head personally, the approval was signed by other relevant leaders. For intercity transportation, he traveled in first-class train seating. The trip was reported as 3 calendar days, with 2 nights in Beijing at RMB 600 per night, and both the intercity transportation invoice and lodging invoice were submitted. A meal subsidy of RMB 300 was claimed. Instead of reimbursing city transportation against receipts, he claimed a lump-sum city transportation allowance of RMB 240. There was no self-driving, car rental, or external unit bearing the related expenses.

case_ch:
In May 2026, Professor Zhang first returned to another city to visit a spouse and then traveled from his hometown to Beijing to participate in a project-related activity. After returning to campus, he sorted out and reimbursed the expenses from these two parallel arrangements together.
For the family visit portion, the reimbursement was reported under the category of a married employee visiting a spouse. This year's arrangement was one round trip, with actual transportation expenses of RMB 950, and the reimbursable amount claimed was also RMB 950. Although the trip was actually made by airplane, the claimed amount was still controlled according to the equivalent train fare for the same route. The materials already included the "Fudan University Faculty and Staff Family Visit Application and Approval Form," with departmental approval completed and the seal of the personnel department affixed. The expenses included only intercity transportation and did not include local transportation expenses.
The subsequent trip to Beijing was charged to research project funds. The "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" had been completed, signed by the unit head, and affixed with the official seal of the department (the first-level budget unit). The traveler was not the unit head personally. For intercity transportation, he traveled in first-class train seating. The itinerary covered 3 calendar days, with 2 nights in Beijing. The lodging expense was reported at RMB 700 per night, and both the intercity transportation invoice and lodging invoice were attached. A meal subsidy of RMB 300 was claimed, and city transportation expenses of RMB 180 were reimbursed against receipts. There was no cost sharing by an external unit, and there was no self-driving or car rental.

case_ch:
In May 2026, Professor Chen traveled to Singapore to attend the International Annual Conference on Education. After returning to China, he submitted both the overseas travel expenses and the expenses of an academic event held on campus during the same period for review together.
For the overseas portion, the expenses were reported under non-research project funds and similar funds. The package included the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" issued by the Exit and Entry Center of the Foreign Affairs Office, as well as the overseas travel expense statement. The expense statement listed the conference registration fee, overseas living expenses, personal incidental allowance, and intercity transportation expenses. The actual mode of transportation was reported under the standard for division-level and lower personnel as economy-class airfare, while the materials also contained standards such as hard-seat train fare and third-class ship cabin. The international airfare was paid by official card, and the lodging expense was paid by bank transfer. Reimbursement was completed in one batch after returning to China. The attached materials clearly included the air ticket and boarding pass. Formal invoices for the lodging expense, conference registration fee, intercity transportation expense, visa fee, and insurance fee were not seen, but all of them had been reviewed and signed by the Exit and Entry Service Center of the Foreign Affairs Office. The appropriate foreign currency was also selected in the Online Reservation Reimbursement system, which converted the amount into RMB.
The on-campus event held during the same period was charged to research project funds. There were about 100 participants, and the event was reported as lasting 3 days. The conference services uniformly arranged lodging and meals. The lodging, meal, and other expenses were reported at RMB 480, RMB 150, and RMB 100 per person per day respectively, for a total of RMB 730 per person per day. The notice, agenda, budget form, detailed list of conference expense expenditures, sign-in sheet of actual attendees, service invoices, original supporting documents, electronic settlement document, and entrustment agreement (contract) were all complete. The entire expense was reimbursed in one batch through the same channel, with no advance payment and no foreign guests in attendance.

case_ch:
In June 2026, the school processed a group of remuneration and conference-related expenses for an academic event, covering both payments for a doctoral dissertation defense and the costs of special lectures and conference organization.
In the remuneration materials, the doctoral dissertation arrangement included 5 reviewers, 5 defense committee members, and 1 defense secretary, with the corresponding standards reported as RMB 300 per person, RMB 300 per person, and RMB 150 per person respectively. During the event, one bureau-director-level person or person of equivalent rank was also invited to deliver a half-day special lecture. The lecture fee was actually calculated as 4 class hours at RMB 900 per class hour. This lecture fee was not specifically stated as being reported on an after-tax basis, and there was no description suggesting that the lecturer taught multiple sessions simultaneously with lecture fees counted repeatedly.
A conference held during the same period was charged to non-research project funds and similar funds and was held in China. There were about 100 participants, and the conference lasted 2 days. The conference services uniformly arranged lodging and meals. The lodging, meal, and other expenses were reported at RMB 300, RMB 200, and RMB 250 per person per day respectively, for a total of RMB 750 per person per day. The conference notice, agenda, budget form, detailed list of conference expense expenditures, sign-in sheet of actual attendees, expense invoices from the conference service provider, original supporting documents, electronic settlement document, and entrustment agreement (contract) were all complete, and the expenses were reimbursed in one batch through the same channel. The venue also separately listed venue rental at RMB 200 per person per day, equipment rental at RMB 100 per person per day, and a cold buffet reception at RMB 220 per person per occasion. There were 10 staff members, including 5 resident staff members. Staff lodging and meals were controlled at RMB 450 per person per day, volunteer subsidies at RMB 100 per person per day, simultaneous interpretation for a non-United Nations official language at RMB 6,000 per interpreter per day, written translation at RMB 200 per 1,000 words, and vehicle use at RMB 1,500 per vehicle per day for a 30-seat bus.

case_ch:
In July 2026, the waterproofing renovation project on the third floor of the laboratory building entered the final settlement stage. The staff member from the Capital Construction Office submitted the project settlement materials together with the related cost review materials.
For the engineering portion, the original approval form was attached, and it bore both the formal seal of the Capital Construction Office and the Project Review Seal of the Fudan University Renovation Management Office. Because the project amount exceeded RMB 100,000, both the head of the Capital Construction Office and the vice president in charge had already signed it. This payment was not an initial payment, but a final settlement payment upon project completion. Therefore, the original "Fudan University Construction Project Cost Review Opinion" and "Fudan University Construction Project Final Settlement Audit Filing Form" were attached. The cost review opinion had already been affixed with the official seal of the cost review company and the "Capital Construction Office Review Process" seal. The filing form also bore the signature and seal of the engineering management department, together with the receipt and processing comments of the Audit Office. The payment amount was handled according to the contract and project progress. There was no excess payment and no advance payment ahead of progress. The final settlement amount was based on the verified total settlement price and did not exceed the contract price.
The same batch of materials also involved an explanation of the cost review fees related to the project. The relevant invoice had originally been issued for the full amount including the warranty retention. The warranty period had now expired, and there was no dispute over project quality during the warranty period. For the refund, the warranty retention receipt issued by the Finance Office had been submitted and signed by the department head. The attached "Fudan University Construction Project Cost Review Opinion" also specified the amount that should be included in the university's construction cost. The claimed amount matched the portion to be borne by the university, the portion to be borne by the construction contractor was separately listed, and a copy of the cost review fee invoice issued in the contractor's name was also provided.

case_ch:
In May 2026, Professor Liu traveled out to coordinate the purchase of a batch of laboratory equipment parts. After returning to campus, he sorted out and reimbursed both the purchase expenses and the transportation and lodging receipts from the same trip together.
For the purchase portion, what was reported was a physical good, but neither its unit price nor its use characteristics met the management standard for fixed assets or intangible assets, and it was not a book, application software, or a batch of similar durable goods. The materials did not include asset capitalization documents such as the "Fudan University Asset Reimbursement Form" (second copy) or the "Fudan University Asset Reimbursement Form (Intangible Assets)" (second copy). Instead, they included the "Fudan University Purchased Inventory Confirmation Form (Based on Review Results)," indicating that this expense was handled as purchased materials.
The itinerary from the same trip was charged to research project funds. The "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" had been completed, signed by the unit head, and affixed with the official seal of the department (the first-level budget unit). For intercity transportation, he traveled in first-class train seating. The trip was reported as 3 calendar days, with 2 nights in Beijing at RMB 600 per night. The intercity transportation invoice, lodging invoice, and city transportation receipts were all provided. A meal subsidy of RMB 300 was claimed, and city transportation expenses of RMB 180 were reimbursed on an actual basis. However, self-driving or car rental was in fact also used on this trip. The car rental fee, fuel cost, and tolls totaled RMB 200, yet no special explanation approved and signed by the project leader was attached.

case_ch:
In June 2026, a peer university visited the campus for exchange and cooperation, and the university organized one set of reimbursement documents for the visitor reception and another for a special conference held during the same period.
The visitor reception was handled on a one-matter, one-settlement basis. The financial invoices, official letter, and "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality List" had all been prepared. The official letter specified the purpose of the visit, the itinerary, and the list of visitors. There were 9 visitors in total and 3 accompanying staff members from our side, and the number of accompanying personnel was kept within the required limit. No lodging expense was claimed separately during the reception. If the visitors needed rest, they were arranged in standard rooms, with no suite for provincial- or ministerial-level personnel involved, and the visitors were not from a local unit in the same city. There was indeed a working meal that day, controlled at RMB 100 per person per day under the boxed meal or buffet standard, and one formal banquet was arranged at RMB 150 per person per occasion. The simple meal standard for accompanying staff was controlled at RMB 50 per person per occasion. Vehicle use was arranged by centralized transportation, with an appropriate vehicle type and a controlled number of accompanying vehicles. However, the "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality Approval Form" was not included in this set of materials.
The special conference held during the same period was charged to research project funds. There were about 100 participants, and the conference lasted 2 days. The conference services uniformly arranged lodging and meals. The lodging, meal, and other expenses were reported at RMB 480, RMB 150, and RMB 100 per person per day respectively, for a total of RMB 730 per person per day. The conference notice, agenda, budget form, detailed list of conference expense expenditures, sign-in sheet of actual attendees, expense invoices provided by the conference service provider, original supporting documents, electronic settlement document, and entrustment agreement (contract) were all complete. The entire expense was reimbursed in one batch through the same channel, with no advance payment and no foreign guests attending.

In April 2026, the school carried out maintenance on a large centrifuge, and the staff member subsequently submitted both the maintenance expenses and the office supplies purchased during the maintenance process for review together.
The maintenance matter had first been applied for through the Asset Management Office system, and the "Fudan University Fixed Asset Maintenance Application Form" had been printed out. The form bore the signatures of the relevant personnel and had been affixed with the official seal of the unit. The original invoice for the maintenance service was also fully attached.
At the same time, the staff member also bought several items of routine office supplies for recording the maintenance ledger, including office stationery, newspapers, and magazines. The amount did not reach the fixed asset threshold and did not fall within the review scope for externally purchased inventory by the Asset Management Office. The seller provided a goods list with the invoice. There was no online shopping transaction record, nor did the case involve books and materials, printing and production, or publication contracts and other such materials.

case_ch:
In August 2026, the university invited a group of overseas partner experts to campus for lectures and exchanges, and the related reception expenses were reimbursed together after the event. The same batch of funding materials also included the receipts from a project team teacher's business trip to Beijing.
For the visitor reception portion, the required filing with the foreign affairs department had already been completed, and the "Fudan University Overseas Expert Work Expense Accounting Report" had been submitted. Both the responsible person and the handling staff member had signed it. The reception list specified the guests' institutions, names, and purposes of the visit. This reimbursement included lodging expenses, intercity transportation expenses, routine meal expenses, banquet expenses, city transportation expenses, gift expenses, and meal allowance subsidies for staff working overtime. The guests were arranged in a four-star hotel under the category for guests of other ranks. This was neither a delegation led by vice-ministerial-level personnel or above nor a reception supporting a conference. The routine meal expenses were reported at RMB 320 per person per day, the banquet at RMB 280 per person per occasion, and gifts were controlled at RMB 180 per person per occasion. There were 4 guests and 4 accompanying staff from our side. The meal allowance subsidy for staff was reported at RMB 50 per person per occasion, and the city transportation arrangement matched the guests' rank.
The Beijing trip included in the same batch of reimbursement was charged to research project funds. The intercity transportation was still reported as first-class train seating, lodging as 2 nights at RMB 600 per night, the meal subsidy as RMB 300, and city transportation expenses on an actual basis at RMB 180. The intercity transportation invoice and lodging invoice were both included, but the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" was not attached.

case_ch:
In June 2026, the library floor repair project entered a new payment stage, and the handling staff member submitted both the warranty retention matter left over from the previous year's project and the current round of project payment materials for review together.
One set of materials concerned the refund of warranty retention. When the original invoice was issued, it had already included the warranty retention in the full amount, and the Finance Office had also issued the corresponding warranty retention receipt. The warranty period had now expired, and no quality dispute had arisen during the warranty period. For this application, the warranty retention receipt had already been attached, and the department head had signed the refund application. However, this set of materials did not include the "Fudan University Construction Project Cost Review Opinion," nor any explanation of the proportion of cost review fees to be borne by the university, and there was also no copy of a cost review fee invoice issued in the name of the construction contractor.
The simultaneously submitted project payment portion involved a project exceeding RMB 100,000. The original approval form, the formal seal of the Capital Construction Office, and the Project Review Seal of the Fudan University Renovation Management Office were all in place, and both the head of the Capital Construction Office and the vice president in charge had signed it. This application was for the initial payment, so the original construction contract was attached. The payment amount was handled according to the contract and project progress, with no excess payment and no advance payment ahead of progress.

case_ch:
In July 2026, during a family visit, Professor Zhao also happened to participate in a long-term training program. After returning, the transportation expenses for the family visit and the training expenditures were separately organized into the same batch of reimbursement materials.
For the family visit portion, the reimbursement was reported under the category of a married employee visiting a spouse. This year's arrangement was one round trip. The actual transportation expense was RMB 950, and the reimbursable amount claimed was also RMB 950. Although the trip was actually made by airplane, the claimed amount was controlled according to the equivalent train fare for the same route. The family visit application form had already completed departmental approval and had been affixed with the seal of the personnel department. The expenses included only intercity transportation and no local transportation expenses.
For the training portion, the expenses were reported under the standard for bureau-level personnel. The training lasted 40 days, of which 10 days exceeded the 30-day threshold. The total of lodging, meal, venue and materials expenses, and other expenses was RMB 29,250, reported respectively as RMB 18,000, RMB 6,750, RMB 3,150, and RMB 1,350. There were no faculty fees during the training, and there were also no lodging, meal, or intercity transportation expenses for any instructor invited from another city. The materials already included the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," the list of trainees and their contact information, and supporting documents such as the detailed expense list provided by the training service provider.

case_ch:
In April 2026, Professor Sun traveled to Japan to attend an international academic conference. After returning to China, he submitted both the overseas travel expenses and a batch of remuneration documents incurred by the school during the same period for review together.
For the overseas portion, the expenses were reported under non-research project funds and similar funds. The package included the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" issued by the Exit and Entry Center of the Foreign Affairs Office, as well as the overseas travel expense statement. The expense statement listed the conference registration fee, overseas living expenses, personal incidental allowance, and intercity transportation expenses. The actual mode of transportation was reported under the standard for division-level and lower personnel as economy-class airfare, together with corresponding standard references such as hard-seat train fare and third-class ship cabin. The international airfare was paid by official card, and the lodging expense was paid by bank transfer. Reimbursement was completed in one batch after returning to China. The attached materials clearly included the air ticket and boarding pass. Formal invoices for the lodging expense, conference registration fee, intercity transportation expense, visa fee, and insurance fee were not seen, but all had already been reviewed and signed by the Exit and Entry Service Center of the Foreign Affairs Office. The corresponding foreign currency was also selected in the Online Reservation Reimbursement system, which converted the amount into RMB.
In the same batch of remuneration materials, the school arranged one doctoral dissertation defense, with 5 reviewers, 5 defense committee members, and 1 defense secretary. The corresponding standards were reported as RMB 300 per person, RMB 300 per person, and RMB 150 per person respectively. In addition, one bureau-director-level person or person of equivalent rank was invited to deliver a half-day special lecture, and the lecture fee was calculated as 4 class hours at RMB 900 per class hour. This lecture fee was not specifically stated as being reported on an after-tax basis, and there was no description of repeated calculation for teaching multiple sessions at the same time.

The seller likewise provided a goods list, and there were no additional materials such as a detailed book list, a fixed asset addition slip, or a publication contract.

case_ch:
In September 2026, the university held an academic seminar in China. After the event, the conference organization expenses were sorted out together, and at the same time, the remuneration materials for experts invited to provide consulting during the event were submitted together.
The conference portion was charged to non-research project funds and similar funds and was held in China. There were about 100 participants, and the conference lasted 2 days. The conference services uniformly arranged lodging and meals. The lodging, meal, and other expenses were reported at RMB 300, RMB 200, and RMB 250 per person per day respectively, for a total of RMB 750 per person per day. The conference notice, agenda, budget form, detailed list of conference expense expenditures, sign-in sheet of actual attendees, service invoices, original supporting documents, electronic settlement document, and entrustment agreement (contract) were all complete, and the entire expense was reimbursed in one batch through the same channel. case_ch:
In May 2026, Professor Li traveled to Beijing for project research. After returning to campus, he organized and reimbursed both the business trip receipts and a set of reception materials arising from exchanges with a partner institution.
The business trip portion was charged to research project funds. The "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" had been completed, signed by the unit head, and affixed with the official seal of the department (the first-level budget unit). For intercity transportation, he traveled in first-class train seating. The itinerary was reported as 3 calendar days, with 2 nights in Beijing at RMB 600 per night, and both the intercity transportation invoice and lodging invoice were attached. A meal subsidy of RMB 300 was claimed. City transportation was not reimbursed against receipts, but a lump-sum city transportation allowance of RMB 500 was directly claimed, and there was no explanation stating that an external unit bore the transportation or lodging expenses.
The reception materials in the same batch were organized on a one-matter, one-settlement basis. The financial invoices, official letter, and "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality List" had all been prepared. There were 9 visitors and 3 accompanying staff members from our side. The working meal was controlled at RMB 100 per person per day under the boxed meal or buffet standard, and one formal banquet at RMB 150 per person per occasion was arranged. No lodging expense was claimed separately. However, the materials did not include the "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality Approval Form."

case_ch:
In April 2026, while updating equipment for classrooms, the school handled both a new purchase of parts and a repair expense for old equipment at the same time.
Although the purchase portion was accompanied by the "Fudan University Purchased Inventory Confirmation Form (Based on Review Results)," the description of the goods themselves did not match the category of purchased materials. The receipts did not clearly show that this was an ordinary consumable item that failed to meet the fixed asset threshold and also did not fall within the management scope of intangible assets. Instead, it looked more like an equipment purchase that should not have been handled as purchased materials.
The maintenance matter submitted in the same batch had already first been applied for through the Asset Management Office system, and the "Fudan University Fixed Asset Maintenance Application Form" had been printed out. The form bore the signatures of the relevant personnel and had been affixed with the official seal of the unit, and the original invoice for the maintenance service was also fully attached.

case_ch:
In July 2026, the school organized a long-term teacher training program, and after the training ended, the training organization expenses were reimbursed together. At the same time, a batch of routine office supplies purchased during conference preparation was also attached afterward.
For the training portion, the expenses were reported under the standard for provincial- or ministerial-level personnel and equivalent personnel. The training lasted 20 days, and the total of lodging, meal, venue and materials expenses, and other expenses was RMB 15,200, including RMB 10,000 for lodging, RMB 3,000 for meals, RMB 1,600 for venue and materials expenses, and RMB 600 for other expenses. During the training period, an instructor from another city was also invited, and the total lecture fees were RMB 2,000. The instructor's lodging, meal, and intercity transportation expenses were reimbursed at RMB 500, RMB 150, and RMB 800 respectively. The related "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," the list of trainees and their contact information, and supporting documents such as the detailed expense list provided by the training service provider were all complete. A written approval was also attached for the portion involving an increased standard.
In the office expense portion of the same batch, the handling staff member also purchased office stationery, newspapers, and magazines. The amount did not reach the fixed asset threshold and did not fall within the review scope for externally purchased inventory by the Asset Management Office. The seller provided a goods list together with the invoice. There was no online shopping transaction record, nor did the case involve books and materials, printing and production, or publication contracts and other such materials.

case_ch:
In August 2026, the university received a group of overseas partner guests on campus to participate in an exchange marking the launch of a joint project. The related reception expenses and the conference service expenses of the launch meeting itself were submitted together after the event.
For the visitor reception portion, the case had been truthfully filed with the foreign affairs department, and the "Fudan University Overseas Expert Work Expense Accounting Report" had been submitted, with both the responsible person and the handling staff member having signed it. The reception list specified the guests' institutions, names, and purposes of the visit. This reimbursement included lodging expenses, intercity transportation expenses, routine meal expenses, banquet expenses, city transportation expenses, gift expenses, and meal allowance subsidies for staff working overtime. The guests were arranged in a four-star hotel under the category for guests of other ranks. This was neither a delegation led by vice-ministerial-level personnel or above nor a conference-supporting reception. Routine meal expenses were reported at RMB 320 per person per day, the banquet at RMB 280 per person per occasion, gifts were controlled at RMB 180 per person per occasion, there were 4 guests and 4 accompanying staff members from our side, the staff meal allowance subsidy was reported at RMB 50 per person per occasion, and the city transportation arrangement matched the guests' rank.
The launch meeting held during the same period was charged to research project funds. There were about 100 participants, and the meeting lasted 2 days. The conference services uniformly arranged lodging and meals. The lodging, meal, and other expenses were reported at RMB 480, RMB 150, and RMB 100 per person per day respectively, for a total of RMB 730 per person per day. The budget form, detailed list of conference expense expenditures, sign-in sheet of actual attendees, service invoices, original supporting documents, electronic settlement document, and entrustment agreement (contract) were all complete, but the conference notice and agenda were not attached.

case_ch:
In May 2026, Professor Zhou traveled overseas to participate in an academic event. After returning to China, he submitted both the overseas travel expenses and the receipts for a domestic trip to Beijing taken before departure to handle visa procedures for review together.
For the overseas portion, the expenses were reported under non-research project funds and similar funds. The applicable transportation standard was still reported under the category for division-level and lower personnel, corresponding to economy-class airfare, hard-seat train fare, and third-class ship cabin. The international airfare was paid by official card, the lodging expense was paid by bank transfer, reimbursement was processed in one batch after returning to China, and the corresponding foreign currency had been selected in the Online Reservation Reimbursement system for conversion into RMB. The air ticket and boarding pass had already been submitted. Formal invoices for the lodging expense, conference registration fee, intercity transportation expense, visa fee, and insurance fee were not provided, but all had been reviewed and signed by the Exit and Entry Service Center of the Foreign Affairs Office. However, this set of materials did not include the overseas travel expense statement, although the other information still specified the conference registration fee, overseas living expenses, personal incidental allowance, and intercity transportation expenses.
The Beijing trip in the same batch was charged to research project funds. The "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" was attached, and because this was a trip taken by the unit head personally, the approval was signed by other relevant leaders. Intercity transportation was in first-class train seating. The itinerary was reported as 3 calendar days, with 2 nights in Beijing at RMB 600 per night, and both the intercity transportation invoice and lodging invoice were submitted. A meal subsidy of RMB 300 was claimed, and a lump-sum city transportation allowance of RMB 240 was claimed. There was no self-driving, car rental, or external unit bearing the expenses.

case_ch:
In June 2026, the school processed both remuneration and training organization expenses together for a teacher training program on research methodology, covering lecture and consulting remuneration as well as the expenditures of the long-term training project itself.
The remuneration materials did not include dissertation review or defense fees, but only special lectures and on-site consulting. The lecturer was reported under the category for division-level and lower personnel. The lecture fees were calculated according to the after-tax standard, totaling 8 class hours at RMB 500 per class hour, equivalent to no more than two half-day sessions. In addition, one bureau-director-level person or person of equivalent rank was arranged to provide on-site consulting for 2 consecutive days at RMB 800 per day. This was conference-related consulting, and there was no case of communication-based consulting charged by project.
The training portion was reported under the standard for bureau-level personnel. The training lasted 40 days, of which 10 days exceeded the 30-day threshold. The total of lodging, meal, venue and materials expenses, and other expenses was RMB 29,250, reported respectively as RMB 18,000, RMB 6,750, RMB 3,150, and RMB 1,350. There were no faculty fees during the training, and there were also no lodging, meal, or intercity transportation expenses for instructors from other cities. The materials already included the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," the list of trainees and their contact information, and the detailed expense list and similar supporting documents provided by the training service provider.

case_ch:
In July 2026, the renovation project for the college conference room entered the settlement stage, and the handling staff member submitted the project settlement materials together with the office supplies purchased in small quantities during construction.
The engineering portion involved a project exceeding RMB 100,000. The original approval form, the formal seal of the Capital Construction Office, and the Project Review Seal of the Fudan University Renovation Management Office were all in place, and both the head of the Capital Construction Office and the vice president in charge had completed their signatures. This application was for final settlement payment upon project completion, so the original "Fudan University Construction Project Cost Review Opinion" and "Fudan University Construction Project Final Settlement Audit Filing Form" were attached. The cost review opinion had been affixed with the official seal of the cost review company and the "Capital Construction Office Review Process" seal, and the filing form also bore the signature and seal of the engineering management department together with the receipt and processing comments of the Audit Office. The payment amount was handled according to the contract and project progress, but the final settlement amount was written as higher than the contract price.
The office expense in the same batch included only office stationery. The seller provided a goods list together with the invoice. There was no online shopping transaction record, nor did the case involve books and materials, printing and production, or publication contracts and other such materials.

case_ch:
In August 2026, after the settlement of a renovation project, the handling staff member placed the materials related to warranty retention together with the explanation of cost review fees in the same batch of reimbursement documents.
For the warranty retention portion, there was already an engineering invoice, and when the invoice was originally issued, it included the warranty retention in the full amount. The Finance Office had issued the corresponding warranty retention receipt, and this receipt was also attached in the current application. The department head had signed the refund application, and up to now there had been no dispute over project quality. However, the warranty period had not yet expired.
The same batch of materials also included a "Fudan University Construction Project Cost Review Opinion," and the claimed amount was written as the portion to be borne by the university. However, this set of cost review fee materials did not include any engineering or equipment invoice, did not explain whether the invoice included warranty retention, and did not provide either the portion to be borne by the construction contractor or a copy of a cost review fee invoice issued in the contractor's name.

In May 2026, a large piece of equipment in the laboratory underwent maintenance, and the handling staff member submitted both the maintenance expenses and the business trip receipts from a simultaneous trip to Beijing to handle project matters.
The maintenance matter had already first been applied for through the Asset Management Office system, and the "Fudan University Fixed Asset Maintenance Application Form" had been printed out. The form bore the signatures of the relevant personnel and had been affixed with the official seal of the unit, and the original invoice for the maintenance service was also fully attached.
The Beijing trip in the same batch was charged to research project funds. The "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" had been completed, signed by the unit head, and affixed with the official seal of the department (the first-level budget unit). Intercity transportation was in first-class train seating. The itinerary was reported as 3 calendar days, with 2 nights in Beijing at RMB 600 per night, and both the intercity transportation invoice and lodging invoice were submitted. A meal subsidy of RMB 300 was claimed. City transportation was not reimbursed against receipts, but a lump-sum city transportation allowance of RMB 500 was directly claimed, and there was no explanation stating that an external unit bore the transportation or lodging expenses.

case_ch:
In April 2026, after returning to campus, Professor Wu organized together the transportation expenses for a family visit and the office supplies temporarily purchased during that visit for reimbursement.
The family visit portion was reported under the category of a married employee visiting parents, but this trip was neither a compliant once-a-year visit to a spouse nor a compliant once-every-four-years visit to parents. The actual transportation expense was RMB 2,100, but the reimbursable amount was filled in as only RMB 500. Although the trip was actually made by airplane, the claimed amount was controlled according to the equivalent train fare for the same route, but only up to RMB 400. The family visit application form had already completed departmental approval and had been affixed with the seal of the personnel department. The expenses included only intercity transportation and no local transportation expenses.
In the office expense portion of the same batch, there were both office stationery and computer accessories. The seller provided a goods list together with the invoice, and there was no online shopping transaction record. This portion was not handled under the category of low-value consumables, and it did not fall within the review scope for externally purchased inventory by the Asset Management Office.

case_ch:
In June 2026, the school received a group of external review experts on campus for exchange and evaluation, and after the event it submitted both the reception expenses and a set of dissertation defense remuneration materials for review together.
The reception portion was organized on a one-matter, one-settlement basis. The financial invoices, official letter, and "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality List" had all been prepared. There were 9 visitors and 3 accompanying staff members from our side. The working meal was controlled at RMB 100 per person per day under the boxed meal or buffet standard, and one formal banquet at RMB 150 per person per occasion was arranged. No lodging expense was claimed separately, and the number of accompanying personnel, the simple meals for staff, and the vehicle arrangements were all controlled according to the rules. However, the materials did not include the "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality Approval Form."
The remuneration materials in the same batch included one doctoral dissertation defense, with 6 reviewers, 6 defense committee members, and 1 defense secretary. In March 2026, the school purchased a new color photocopying machine and submitted the equipment capitalization materials together with the office consumables purchased during the subsequent trial operation stage.
The equipment purchase portion corresponded to a physical good with a unit price exceeding RMB 1,000. The "Fudan University Asset Reimbursement Form" (second copy) had been attached, and fixed asset registration had already been completed through the Asset Management Office. It did not involve library registration and was not handled under the category of purchased materials or intangible assets.
In the office expense portion of the same batch, the handling staff member also purchased office stationery, newspapers, and magazines. The amount did not reach the fixed asset threshold and did not fall within the review scope for externally purchased inventory by the Asset Management Office. The seller provided a goods list together with the invoice. There was no online shopping transaction record, nor did the case involve books and materials, printing and production, or publication contracts and other such materials.

In May 2026, Professor Zheng attended an academic conference, and after returning to campus, he submitted both the conference-organization-related materials and the receipts from his own simultaneous business trip to Beijing for review together.
The conference portion was charged to research project funds. There were about 100 participants, and the conference lasted 2 days. The conference services uniformly arranged lodging and meals. The lodging, meal, and other expenses were reported at RMB 500, RMB 150, and RMB 110 per person per day respectively, while the total was written as RMB 760 per person per day. The conference notice, agenda, budget form, detailed list of conference expense expenditures, sign-in sheet of actual attendees, service invoices, original supporting documents, electronic settlement document, and entrustment agreement (contract) were all submitted together. The entire expense was reimbursed in one batch through the same channel.
The Beijing trip in the same batch was also charged to research project funds. Intercity transportation was still reported as first-class train seating, lodging as 2 nights at RMB 600 per night, the meal subsidy as RMB 300, and city transportation expenses on an actual basis at RMB 180. Both the intercity transportation invoice and the lodging invoice were included, but the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" was not attached.

case_ch:
In August 2026, Professor Lin traveled to the United Kingdom to participate in a teaching methodology training program, and after returning to China, submitted both the overseas travel expenses and the training expenditures together.
For the overseas portion, the expenses were reported under non-research project funds and similar funds. The applicable transportation standard was still reported under the category for division-level and lower personnel, corresponding to economy-class airfare, hard-seat train fare, and third-class ship cabin. The international airfare was paid by official card, the lodging expense was paid by bank transfer, reimbursement was processed in one batch after returning to China, and the corresponding foreign currency had been selected in the Online Reservation Reimbursement system for conversion into RMB. The air ticket and boarding pass had already been submitted. Formal invoices for the lodging expense, conference registration fee, intercity transportation expense, visa fee, and insurance fee were not provided, but all had been reviewed and signed by the Exit and Entry Service Center of the Foreign Affairs Office. However, this set of materials did not include the overseas travel expense statement, although the other information still specified the conference registration fee, overseas living expenses, personal incidental allowance, and intercity transportation expenses.
The training portion was reported under the standard for bureau-level personnel. The training lasted 25 days and did not exceed 30 days. The total of lodging, meal, venue and materials expenses, and other expenses was RMB 16,250, reported respectively as RMB 10,000, RMB 3,750, RMB 1,750, and RMB 750. There were no faculty fees during the training, and there were also no lodging, meal, or intercity transportation expenses for instructors from other cities. The materials already included the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," the list of trainees and their contact information, and the detailed expense list and similar supporting documents provided by the training service provider.

case_ch:
In March 2026, the university received a group of guests from external institutions for visits and exchange, and the related reception expenses were organized together with remuneration for an on-site lecture after the event for reimbursement.
The reception portion was reported under the category of domestic visitor reception, but the case had also been truthfully filed with the foreign affairs department, and the "Fudan University Overseas Expert Work Expense Accounting Report" had been submitted, with both the responsible person and the handling staff member having signed it. The reception list specified the guests' institutions, names, and purposes of the visit. This reimbursement included lodging expenses, intercity transportation expenses, routine meal expenses, banquet expenses, city transportation expenses, gift expenses, and meal allowance subsidies for staff working overtime. The guests were arranged in a five-star hotel under the standard for ministerial- or vice-ministerial-level guests, and the visiting delegation was led by vice-ministerial-level personnel or above. Routine meal expenses were reported at RMB 500 per person per day, the banquet at RMB 400 per person per occasion, gifts were controlled at RMB 400 per person per occasion, there were 4 guests and 4 accompanying staff members from our side, the staff meal allowance subsidy was reported at RMB 50 per person per occasion, the city transportation arrangement matched the guests' rank, and this was not a conference-supporting reception.
The remuneration materials in the same batch included neither dissertation review or defense fees nor expert consulting fees, but only one special lecture. The lecturer was reported under the category of bureau-director-level personnel and persons of equivalent rank. The lecture fee was calculated according to the after-tax standard, totaling 4 class hours at RMB 1,200 per class hour, corresponding to no more than a half-day teaching load.

case_ch:
In May 2026, a renovation project under the General Affairs Office entered the settlement stage, and the handling staff member submitted the project settlement materials together with the receipts for a simultaneous inspection trip.
The engineering portion involved a project exceeding RMB 100,000. The original approval form, the formal seal of the Capital Construction Office, and the Project Review Seal of the Fudan University Renovation Management Office were all in place, and both the head of the Capital Construction Office and the vice president in charge had completed their signatures. This application was for final settlement payment upon project completion, so the original "Fudan University Construction Project Cost Review Opinion" and "Fudan University Construction Project Final Settlement Audit Filing Form" were attached. The cost review opinion had been affixed with the official seal of the cost review company and the "Capital Construction Office Review Process" seal, and the filing form also bore the signature and seal of the engineering management department together with the receipt and processing comments of the Audit Office. The payment amount was handled according to the contract and project progress, but the final settlement amount was written as higher than the contract price.
The itinerary in the same batch was charged to research project funds. The "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" had been completed, signed by the unit head, and affixed with the official seal of the department (the first-level budget unit), and the traveler was not the unit head personally. case_ch:
In June 2026, while a certain engineering project was still within its warranty period, the handling staff member organized together the materials related to warranty retention and the transportation and lodging receipts for an invited external person coming to campus to handle affairs.
For the warranty retention portion, there was already an engineering invoice, and when the invoice was originally issued, it included the warranty retention in the full amount. The Finance Office had issued the corresponding warranty retention receipt, and this receipt was also attached in the current application. The department head had signed the refund application, and up to now there had been no dispute over project quality. However, the warranty period had not yet expired, and the materials also did not include the "Fudan University Construction Project Cost Review Opinion," any explanation of the proportion to be borne by the university, or a copy of a cost review fee invoice issued in the contractor's name.
The transportation and lodging expenses for coming to campus in the same batch were charged to research project funds. The "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" was attached and had been signed by the unit head and affixed with the official seal of the department (the first-level budget unit). Intercity transportation was in first-class train seating. The itinerary was reported as 3 calendar days, with 2 nights in Beijing at RMB 600 per night. The intercity transportation invoice, lodging invoice, and city transportation receipts were all complete. A meal subsidy of RMB 300 was claimed, and city transportation expenses of RMB 180 were reimbursed on an actual basis. However, the actual reimbursement recipient this time was an invited external person. Since the transportation and lodging standard had been confirmed by the project leader's signature, no meal subsidy or city transportation allowance should have been paid.

case_ch:
In July 2026, during a family visit, Professor Qian made a detour to attend a short-term workshop, and after returning to campus, submitted both the training expenditures and the family visit transportation expenses for review together.
The training portion was reported under the standard for division-level and lower personnel. The training lasted 25 days, and the total of lodging, meal, venue and materials expenses, and other expenses was RMB 16,950, including RMB 8,500 for lodging, RMB 3,250 for meals, RMB 1,250 for venue and materials expenses, and RMB 750 for other expenses. During the training period, an instructor from another city was invited, and the total lecture fees were RMB 1,200. The instructor's lodging, meal, and intercity transportation expenses were reimbursed at RMB 340, RMB 130, and RMB 500 respectively, and written approval was also attached for the portion involving an increased standard. However, although the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures" and the list of trainees had both been prepared, the invoices provided by the training service provider were not submitted.
The family visit portion was reported under the category of an unmarried employee visiting parents. This year's arrangement was one round trip. The actual transportation expense was RMB 800, and the reimbursable amount claimed was also RMB 800. The family visit application form had already completed departmental approval and had been affixed with the seal of the personnel department. The expenses included only intercity transportation and no local transportation expenses, and there was no case of controlling the claimed amount according to airfare.

case_ch:
In April 2026, the university simultaneously organized both visitor reception materials and conference organization expenses for a work exchange activity.
The reception portion was handled on a one-matter, one-settlement basis. The financial invoices, official letter, "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality List," and "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality Approval Form" were all complete. The official letter specified the purpose of the visit, itinerary, and list of visitors. There were 9 visitors and 3 accompanying staff members from our side. No lodging expense was claimed separately during the reception. If the visitors needed rest, they were arranged in standard rooms, with no suite for provincial- or ministerial-level personnel involved, and the visitors were not from a local unit in the same city. There was indeed a working meal that day, controlled at RMB 100 per person per day under the boxed meal or buffet standard, and one formal banquet was arranged at RMB 150 per person per occasion. The simple meal standard for accompanying staff was controlled at RMB 50 per person per occasion, and vehicle use was arranged by centralized transportation, with an appropriate vehicle type and a controlled number of accompanying vehicles.
The conference portion held during the same period was charged to research project funds. There were about 100 participants, and the conference lasted 2 days. The conference services uniformly arranged lodging and meals. The lodging, meal, and other expenses were reported at RMB 500, RMB 150, and RMB 110 per person per day respectively, while the total was written as RMB 760 per person per day. The conference notice, agenda, budget form, detailed list of conference expense expenditures, sign-in sheet of actual attendees, service invoices, original supporting documents, electronic settlement document, and entrustment agreement (contract) were all submitted together. The entire expense was reimbursed in one batch through the same channel.

case_ch:
In August 2026, Professor Shen traveled overseas to participate in an academic event, and after returning to China, submitted both the overseas travel expenses and a batch of small office items purchased before departure for review together.
For the overseas portion, the expenses were reported under non-research project funds and similar funds. The package included the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" issued by the Exit and Entry Center of the Foreign Affairs Office, together with the overseas travel expense statement. The expense statement specified the conference registration fee, overseas living expenses, personal incidental allowance, and intercity transportation expenses. The actual mode of transportation was reported under the standard for division-level and lower personnel as economy-class airfare, together with corresponding standard references such as hard-seat train fare and third-class ship cabin. The international airfare was paid by official card, the lodging expense was paid by bank transfer, and reimbursement was completed in one batch after returning to China. The attached materials clearly included the air ticket and boarding pass. Formal invoices for the lodging expense, conference registration fee, intercity transportation expense, visa fee, and insurance fee were not seen, but all had already been reviewed and signed by the Exit and Entry Service Center of the Foreign Affairs Office. The corresponding foreign currency had also been selected in the Online Reservation Reimbursement system, which converted the amount into RMB.
The office expense in the same batch included only office stationery. The seller provided a goods list together with the invoice, and there was no online shopping transaction record. However, in order to avoid reaching the threshold for a required review list, the invoice amounts were artificially split across invoices issued during the same period.

case_ch:
In May 2026, the school processed a batch of academic support expenses, including both remuneration for a short special lecture and the purchase of supporting office consumables.
The remuneration materials included neither dissertation review or defense fees nor expert consulting fees, but only one special lecture. The lecturer was reported under the category for division-level and lower personnel. The lecture fee was actually calculated as 2 class hours at RMB 400 per class hour. This lecture fee was not specifically stated as being reported on an after-tax basis, and it corresponded to no more than a half-day teaching load.
In the office expense portion of the same batch, there were both office stationery and computer accessories. The amount did not reach the fixed asset threshold and did not fall within the review scope for externally purchased inventory by the Asset Management Office. The seller provided a goods list together with the invoice. There was no online shopping transaction record, nor did the case involve books and materials, printing and production, or publication contracts and other such materials.

case_ch:
In June 2026, after the laboratory electrical renovation project was launched, the handling staff member submitted both the initial payment materials for the project and the maintenance receipts for an incubator for review together.
The engineering portion involved a project exceeding RMB 100,000. The original approval form, the formal seal of the Capital Construction Office, and the Project Review Seal of the Fudan University Renovation Management Office were all in place, and both the head of the Capital Construction Office and the vice president in charge had completed their signatures. This application was for the initial payment, so the original construction contract was attached. The payment amount was handled according to the contract and project progress, with no excess payment and no advance payment ahead of progress.
The maintenance matter in the same batch had already first been applied for through the Asset Management Office system, and the "Fudan University Fixed Asset Maintenance Application Form" had been printed out. The form bore the signatures of the relevant personnel and had been affixed with the official seal of the unit, and the original invoice for the maintenance service was also fully attached.

case_ch:
In August 2026, the university received a delegation from external institutions for exchange, and after the event it organized together the reception expenses and the office expenses used for venue setup for reimbursement.
The reception portion had already been truthfully filed with the foreign affairs department, and the "Fudan University Overseas Expert Work Expense Accounting Report" had been submitted, with both the responsible person and the handling staff member having signed it. The reception list specified the guests' institutions, names, and purposes of the visit. This reimbursement included lodging expenses, intercity transportation expenses, routine meal expenses, banquet expenses, city transportation expenses, gift expenses, and meal allowance subsidies for staff working overtime. The guests were arranged in a four-star hotel under the category for guests of other ranks. This was neither a delegation led by vice-ministerial-level personnel or above nor a conference-supporting reception. Routine meal expenses were reported at RMB 280 per person per day, the banquet at RMB 280 per person per occasion, gifts were controlled at RMB 260 per person per occasion, there were 4 guests and 4 accompanying staff members from our side, the staff meal allowance subsidy was reported at RMB 50 per person per occasion, and the city transportation arrangement matched the guests' rank.
The office expense in the same batch included only office stationery. The seller provided a goods list together with the invoice. There was no online shopping transaction record, nor did the case involve books and materials, printing and production, or publication contracts and other such materials.

case_ch:
In May 2026, Professor He first traveled to Beijing on business, and after the trip ended, made a detour back to his hometown to visit his parents. After returning to campus, he submitted together the receipts for both segments of travel for review.
The business trip portion was charged to research project funds. The "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" had been completed, signed by the unit head, and affixed with the official seal of the department (the first-level budget unit), and the traveler was not the unit head personally. Intercity transportation was in first-class train seating. The itinerary was reported as 3 calendar days, with 2 nights in Beijing at RMB 600 per night. The lodging invoice and city transportation receipts were both provided, a meal subsidy of RMB 300 was claimed, and city transportation expenses of RMB 180 were reimbursed on an actual basis. However, the intercity transportation invoice was missing, and there was no special explanation for being unable to obtain transportation receipts in a remote area.
The family visit portion was reported under the category of a married employee visiting parents. This trip belonged to the once-every-four-years family visit arrangement. The actual transportation expense was RMB 2,000, but the reimbursable amount was filled in as only RMB 350. The family visit application form had already completed departmental approval and had been affixed with the seal of the personnel department. The expenses included only intercity transportation and no local transportation expenses, and there was no case of controlling the amount according to airfare.

case_ch:
In September 2026, Professor Huang invited experts to campus for lectures and degree-defense-related activities, and after the event organized together the remuneration receipts and the reception expenses for reimbursement.
The remuneration materials included a master's dissertation defense, lecture fees, and communication-based consulting fees. There were 3 master's dissertation reviewers, 5 defense committee members, and 1 defense secretary, and the corresponding standards were reported as RMB 300 per person, RMB 300 per person, and RMB 100 per person respectively. In addition, one academician was invited to give a half-day special lecture. The lecture fee was calculated according to the after-tax standard, totaling 4 class hours at RMB 1,400 per class hour, and there was no case of repeated calculation for teaching multiple sessions at the same time. At the same time, expert consulting was also carried out by communication, paid at RMB 80 per project rather than by person-day.
The reception portion had already been truthfully filed with the foreign affairs department, and the "Fudan University Overseas Expert Work Expense Accounting Report" had been submitted, with both the responsible person and the handling staff member having signed it. The reception list specified the guests' institutions, names, and purposes of the visit. This reimbursement included lodging expenses, intercity transportation expenses, routine meal expenses, banquet expenses, city transportation expenses, gift expenses, and meal allowance subsidies for staff working overtime. The guests were arranged in a four-star hotel under the category for guests of other ranks. This was neither a delegation led by vice-ministerial-level personnel or above nor a conference-supporting reception. Routine meal expenses were reported at RMB 280 per person per day, the banquet at RMB 280 per person per occasion, gifts were controlled at RMB 180 per person per occasion, and there were 4 guests and 4 accompanying staff members from our side. However, the meal allowance subsidy for staff was reported at RMB 80 per person per occasion, which was higher than the normal control standard.

In June 2026, the university held an academic conference, and after the event submitted together the conference organization expenses and a separate set of official hospitality materials incurred during the conference.
The conference portion was charged to research project funds. There were about 100 participants, and the conference lasted 2 days. Conference services uniformly arranged lodging, but meals were not uniformly arranged. Lodging expenses were reported at RMB 500 per person per day, and after combining meal and other expenses, the total was converted to about RMB 650 per person per day. The conference notice, agenda, budget form, detailed list of conference expense expenditures, sign-in sheet of actual attendees, service invoices, original supporting documents, electronic settlement document, and entrustment agreement (contract) were all complete, and the entire expense was reimbursed in one batch through the same channel.
The reception materials in the same batch were organized on a one-matter, one-settlement basis. The financial invoices, official letter, and "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality List" had all been prepared. There were 9 visitors and 3 accompanying staff members from our side. The working meal was controlled at RMB 100 per person per day under the boxed meal or buffet standard, and one formal banquet at RMB 150 per person per occasion was arranged. No lodging expense was claimed separately. However, the materials did not include the "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality Approval Form."

In July 2026, after a training program organized by the school ended, the training expenses were organized together, and at the same time the maintenance receipts for teaching equipment that malfunctioned during the training were also submitted for review.
The training portion was reported under the standard for division-level and lower personnel. The training lasted 25 days, and the total of lodging, meal, venue and materials expenses, and other expenses was RMB 16,950, including RMB 8,500 for lodging, RMB 3,250 for meals, RMB 1,250 for venue and materials expenses, and RMB 750 for other expenses. During the training period, an instructor from another city was invited, and the total lecture fees were RMB 1,200. The instructor's lodging, meal, and intercity transportation expenses were reimbursed at RMB 340, RMB 130, and RMB 500 respectively, and written approval was also attached for the portion involving an increased standard. However, although the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures" and the list of trainees were both complete, the invoices provided by the training service provider were not submitted.
The maintenance matter in the same batch had already first been applied for through the Asset Management Office system, and the "Fudan University Fixed Asset Maintenance Application Form" had been printed out. The form bore the signatures of the relevant personnel and had been affixed with the official seal of the unit, and the original invoice for the maintenance service was also fully attached.

case_ch:
In May 2026, after returning to campus from an overseas visit, a teacher from our university submitted together both his own overseas travel expenses and the reception materials for a follow-up return visit to campus by the partner institution for review.
For the overseas portion, the expenses were reported under non-research project funds and similar funds. The package included the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" issued by the Exit and Entry Center of the Foreign Affairs Office and the overseas travel expense statement. The expense statement specified the conference registration fee, overseas living expenses, personal incidental allowance, and intercity transportation expenses. The actual mode of transportation was reported under the standard for division-level and lower personnel as economy-class airfare, together with corresponding standard references such as hard-seat train fare and third-class ship cabin. The international airfare was paid by official card, the lodging expense was paid by bank transfer, and reimbursement was completed in one batch after returning to China. The attached materials clearly included the air ticket, boarding pass, and lodging invoice. Formal invoices for the conference registration fee, intercity transportation expense, visa fee, and insurance fee were not seen, but all had already been reviewed and signed by the Exit and Entry Service Center of the Foreign Affairs Office. The corresponding foreign currency had also been selected in the Online Reservation Reimbursement system, which converted the amount into RMB.
The reception portion for the return visit had already been truthfully filed with the foreign affairs department, and the "Fudan University Overseas Expert Work Expense Accounting Report" had been submitted, with both the responsible person and the handling staff member having signed it. The reception list specified the guests' institutions, names, and purposes of the visit. This reimbursement included lodging expenses, intercity transportation expenses, routine meal expenses, banquet expenses, city transportation expenses, gift expenses, and meal allowance subsidies for staff working overtime. The guests were arranged in a four-star hotel under the category for guests of other ranks. This was neither a delegation led by vice-ministerial-level personnel or above nor a conference-supporting reception. Routine meal expenses were reported at RMB 320 per person per day, the banquet at RMB 280 per person per occasion, gifts were controlled at RMB 180 per person per occasion, there were 4 guests and 4 accompanying staff members from our side, the staff meal allowance subsidy was reported at RMB 50 per person per occasion, and the city transportation arrangement matched the guests' rank.

case_ch:
In August 2026, after the warranty period of a certain project expired, the refund of warranty retention began to be processed, and the handling staff member submitted together the warranty retention materials and the receipts for office supplies purchased while organizing the project archives.
For the warranty retention portion, there was already an engineering invoice, and when the invoice was originally issued, it included the warranty retention in the full amount. The Finance Office had issued the corresponding warranty retention receipt, and this receipt was also attached in the current application. The department head had signed the refund application, and the warranty period had expired with no quality dispute arising during that time. The claimed amount in the materials was written as the portion to be borne by the university, while the portion to be borne by the construction contractor was listed separately, and a copy of an invoice in the contractor's name was also attached.
The office expense in the same batch included only office stationery. The seller provided a goods list together with the invoice, and there was no online shopping transaction record. However, in order to avoid reaching the threshold for a required review list, the invoice amounts were artificially split across invoices issued during the same period. No "Fudan University Construction Project Cost Review Opinion" was included in that refund or warranty-retention set of materials.

case_ch:
In May 2026, the school organized a batch of remuneration materials for a project review activity and simultaneously submitted the business trip receipts of another teacher traveling to Beijing.
The remuneration materials included neither dissertation review or defense fees nor expert consulting fees, but only one special lecture. The lecturer was reported under the category for division-level and lower personnel. The lecture fee was actually calculated as 2 class hours at RMB 400 per class hour. This lecture fee was not specifically stated as being reported on an after-tax basis, and it corresponded to no more than a half-day teaching load.
The Beijing trip in the same batch was charged to research project funds. The "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" was attached, and because this was a trip taken by the unit head personally, the approval was signed by other relevant leaders. Intercity transportation was in first-class train seating. The itinerary was reported as 3 calendar days, with 2 nights in Beijing at RMB 600 per night, and both the intercity transportation invoice and lodging invoice were submitted. A meal subsidy of RMB 300 was claimed, and a lump-sum city transportation allowance of RMB 240 was claimed. There was no self-driving, car rental, or external unit bearing the expenses.
