case_ch:
In April 2026, when the school was receiving a visiting unit, it temporarily purchased a batch of bottled water and disposable paper cups, and after the event organized together this office expense and the visitor reception materials for reimbursement.
In the office expense portion, there were both office stationery and drinking water. The amount did not reach the fixed asset threshold and did not fall within the review scope for externally purchased inventory by the Asset Management Office. The seller provided a goods list together with the invoice. There was no online shopping transaction record, nor did the case involve books and materials, printing and production, or publication contracts and other such materials.
The reception materials in the same batch were reported under the category of domestic visitor reception, but the case had also been truthfully filed with the foreign affairs department, and the "Fudan University Overseas Expert Work Expense Accounting Report" had been submitted, with both the responsible person and the handling staff member having signed it. The reception list specified the guests' institutions, names, and purposes of the visit. This reimbursement included lodging expenses, intercity transportation expenses, routine meal expenses, banquet expenses, city transportation expenses, gift expenses, and meal allowance subsidies for staff working overtime. The guests were arranged in a four-star hotel under the category for guests of other ranks. Routine meal expenses were reported at RMB 280 per person per day, the banquet at RMB 280 per person per occasion, gifts were controlled at RMB 180 per person per occasion, there were 4 guests and 4 accompanying staff members from our side, and the staff meal allowance subsidy was reported at RMB 50 per person per occasion. However, this visit itself was a conference-supporting activity, and the reception materials also simultaneously included conference-supporting lodging or meal expenses.

case_ch:
In May 2026, Professor Wang traveled out to attend an annual academic conference, and after returning to campus, submitted together his own business trip receipts, the conference organization materials, and the office supplies purchased in small quantities before the conference for review.
The business trip portion was charged to research project funds. The "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" had been completed, signed by the unit head, and affixed with the official seal of the department (the first-level budget unit), and the traveler was not the unit head personally. Intercity transportation was in first-class train seating. The itinerary was reported as 3 calendar days, with 2 nights in Beijing at RMB 600 per night. The intercity transportation invoice, lodging invoice, and city transportation receipts were all complete. A meal subsidy of RMB 300 was claimed, and city transportation expenses of RMB 180 were reimbursed on an actual basis. There was no cost sharing by an external unit, and there was no self-driving or car rental.
The conference portion during the same period was charged to research project funds. There were about 100 participants, and the conference lasted 2 days. The conference services uniformly arranged lodging and meals. The lodging, meal, and other expenses were reported at RMB 500, RMB 150, and RMB 110 per person per day respectively, while the total was written as RMB 760 per person per day. The conference notice, agenda, budget form, detailed list of conference expense expenditures, sign-in sheet of actual attendees, service invoices, original supporting documents, electronic settlement document, and entrustment agreement (contract) were all submitted together. The entire expense was reimbursed in one batch through the same channel.
The office expense in the same batch included only office stationery. The seller provided a goods list together with the invoice, and there was no online shopping transaction record. However, in order to avoid reaching the threshold for a required review list, the invoice amounts were artificially split across invoices issued during the same period.

case_ch:
In July 2026, Professor Li participated in a training program for key university finance personnel, and after returning to campus, organized together the training expenditures, the receipts from a simultaneous trip, and the office supplies purchased while preparing the materials before the event for reimbursement.
The training portion was reported under the standard for provincial- or ministerial-level personnel and equivalent personnel. The training lasted 20 days, and the total of lodging, meal, venue and materials expenses, and other expenses was RMB 18,720, including RMB 11,000 for lodging, RMB 3,300 for meals, RMB 1,760 for venue and materials expenses, and RMB 660 for other expenses. During the training period, an instructor from another city was invited, and the total lecture fees were RMB 2,500. The instructor's lodging, meal, and intercity transportation expenses were reimbursed at RMB 600, RMB 200, and RMB 900 respectively. The "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," the list of trainees and their contact information, and the detailed expense list and similar supporting documents provided by the training service provider were all complete, but no separate written approval was attached for the portion involving the increased standard.
The itinerary in the same batch was charged to research project funds. Intercity transportation was still reported as first-class train seating, lodging as 2 nights at RMB 600 per night, the meal subsidy as RMB 300, and city transportation expenses on an actual basis at RMB 180. Both the intercity transportation invoice and the lodging invoice were included, but the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" was not attached.
In addition, some office stationery and computer accessories were purchased. The amount did not reach the fixed asset threshold and did not fall within the review scope for externally purchased inventory by the Asset Management Office. The seller provided a goods list together with the invoice. There was no online shopping transaction record, nor did the case involve books and materials, printing and production, or publication contracts and other such materials.

case_ch:
In May 2026, Professor Zhang first returned to his hometown for a family visit and then traveled onward to another city to participate in a project review activity. After returning to campus, he submitted together the family visit transportation expenses, the travel itinerary, and the remuneration materials from the same period for review.
The family visit portion was reported under the category of an unmarried employee visiting parents. This year's arrangement was one round trip. The actual transportation expense was RMB 700, and the reimbursable amount claimed was also RMB 700. The expenses included only intercity transportation and no local transportation expenses, and there was no case of controlling the claimed amount according to airfare. However, this set of materials did not include the family visit application form.
The itinerary was charged to research project funds. The "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" was attached, and because this was a trip taken by the unit head personally, the approval was signed by other relevant leaders. Intercity transportation was in first-class train seating. The itinerary was reported as 3 calendar days, with 2 nights in Beijing at RMB 600 per night, and both the intercity transportation invoice and lodging invoice were submitted. A meal subsidy of RMB 300 was claimed, and a lump-sum city transportation allowance of RMB 240 was claimed. There was no self-driving, car rental, or external unit bearing the expenses.
The remuneration materials in the same batch included neither dissertation review or defense fees nor expert consulting fees, but only one special lecture. The lecturer was reported under the category of bureau-director-level personnel and persons of equivalent rank. The lecture fee was calculated according to the after-tax standard, totaling 4 class hours at RMB 1,200 per class hour, corresponding to no more than a half-day teaching load.

case_ch:
In September 2026, Professor Chen traveled to Germany to participate in an international education event, and after returning to China, submitted together the overseas travel expenses, the conference service materials for an on-campus management meeting held during the same period, and a software purchase receipt.
For the overseas portion, the expenses were reported under research project funds and similar funds. The package included the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" issued by the Exit and Entry Center of the Foreign Affairs Office and the overseas travel expense statement. The expense statement specified the conference registration fee, overseas living expenses, personal incidental allowance, and intercity transportation expenses. The applicable transportation standard was reported under the category for bureau-director-level and above personnel as first-class airfare, together with corresponding information such as hard-seat train fare and third-class ship cabin. The international airfare was paid by official card, the lodging expense was paid by bank transfer, and reimbursement was completed in one batch after returning to China. The air ticket and boarding pass had already been submitted. The remaining types of invoices were not seen in formal form, but all had been reviewed and signed by the Exit and Entry Service Center of the Foreign Affairs Office. The corresponding foreign currency had also been selected in the Online Reservation Reimbursement system, which converted the amount into RMB.
The conference portion during the same period was charged to non-research project funds and similar funds and belonged to a domestic management conference. The conference lasted 1 day. Lodging, meal, and other expenses were reported at RMB 340, RMB 130, and RMB 90 per person per day respectively, while the total was written as RMB 560 per person per day. The conference notice, agenda, budget form, detailed list of conference expense expenditures, sign-in sheet of actual attendees, service invoices, original supporting documents, electronic settlement document, and entrustment agreement (contract) were all complete, and the entire expense was reimbursed in one batch through the same channel.
Another set of purchase materials was recorded as intangible assets, specifically application software, and was accompanied by the "Fudan University Asset Reimbursement Form (Intangible Assets)" (second copy). However, the receipt also stated that the value was below RMB 2,000, so it did not in fact meet the management standard for intangible assets.

case_ch:
In June 2026, the full renovation project for the restrooms in a teaching building entered the settlement stage, and the handling staff member submitted together the project settlement materials, the materials related to warranty retention, and the office supplies purchased in small quantities during construction.
The engineering portion involved a project exceeding RMB 100,000. The original approval form, the formal seal of the Capital Construction Office, and the Project Review Seal of the Fudan University Renovation Management Office were all in place, and both the head of the Capital Construction Office and the vice president in charge had completed their signatures. This application was for final settlement payment upon project completion, so the original "Fudan University Construction Project Cost Review Opinion" and "Fudan University Construction Project Final Settlement Audit Filing Form" were attached. The cost review opinion had been affixed with the official seal of the cost review company and the "Capital Construction Office Review Process" seal, and the filing form also bore the signature and seal of the engineering management department together with the receipt and processing comments of the Audit Office. However, the final settlement amount was written as higher than the contract price.
The warranty retention materials in the same batch already included an engineering invoice, and when it was issued, it included the warranty retention in the full amount. The Finance Office had issued the corresponding warranty retention receipt, and this receipt was also attached in the current application. The department head had signed the refund application, and up to now there had been no dispute over project quality. However, the warranty period had not yet expired, and the materials also did not include the explanation of the university's burden-sharing proportion corresponding to the "Fudan University Construction Project Cost Review Opinion" or a copy of a cost review fee invoice issued in the contractor's name.
There was also another office expense in the same batch that included office stationery and office furniture. The amount did not reach the fixed asset threshold and did not fall within the review scope for externally purchased inventory by the Asset Management Office. The seller provided a goods list together with the invoice.

case_ch:
In May 2026, the laboratory arranged for an engineer from the manufacturer to come to campus to repair an NMR device, and after the event submitted together the maintenance expenses, the engineer's transportation and lodging receipts for coming to campus, and a set of reception materials for review.
Although the maintenance matter had already had the "Fudan University Fixed Asset Maintenance Application Form" printed out, and the form bore the signatures of the relevant personnel and had been affixed with the official seal of the unit, and the original invoice for the maintenance service was also fully attached, the application had not actually been completed in advance through the Asset Management Office system.
The transportation and lodging portion in the same batch was charged to research project funds. The "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" was attached and had been signed by the unit head and affixed with the official seal of the department (the first-level budget unit). Intercity transportation was in first-class train seating. The itinerary was reported as 3 calendar days, with 2 nights in Beijing at RMB 600 per night. The intercity transportation invoice, lodging invoice, and city transportation receipts were all complete. A meal subsidy of RMB 300 was claimed, and city transportation expenses of RMB 180 were reimbursed on an actual basis. However, the actual reimbursement recipient this time was an invited external person. Since the transportation and lodging standard had been confirmed by the project leader's signature, no meal subsidy or city transportation allowance should have been paid.
The reception materials were organized on a one-matter, one-settlement basis. The financial invoices, official letter, "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality List," and "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality Approval Form" were all complete. There were 9 visitors and 3 accompanying staff members from our side, and no lodging expense was claimed separately. The working meal that day was reported at RMB 120 per person per day, which exceeded the usual upper limit, while the formal banquet was controlled at RMB 150 per person per occasion.

case_ch:
In April 2026, the university simultaneously organized three sets of materials for a disciplinary development seminar: visitor reception, conference organization, and the purchase of small office supplies.
The visitor reception portion was reported under the category of domestic visitor reception, but the case had also been truthfully filed with the foreign affairs department, and the "Fudan University Overseas Expert Work Expense Accounting Report" had been submitted, with both the responsible person and the handling staff member having signed it. The reception list specified the guests' institutions, names, and purposes of the visit. This reimbursement included lodging expenses, intercity transportation expenses, routine meal expenses, banquet expenses, city transportation expenses, gift expenses, and meal allowance subsidies for staff working overtime. The guests were arranged in a four-star hotel under the category for guests of other ranks. Routine meal expenses were reported at RMB 280 per person per day, the banquet at RMB 280 per person per occasion, gifts were controlled at RMB 180 per person per occasion, there were 4 guests and 4 accompanying staff members from our side, and the staff meal allowance subsidy was reported at RMB 50 per person per occasion. However, this visit itself was a conference-supporting activity, and the reception materials simultaneously included conference-supporting lodging or meal expenses.
The conference portion during the same period was charged to research project funds. The conference lasted 2 days, and conference services uniformly arranged lodging but did not uniformly arrange meals. Lodging expenses were reported at RMB 500 per person per day, and after combining meal and other expenses, the total was converted to about RMB 650 per person per day. The conference notice, agenda, budget form, detailed list of conference expense expenditures, sign-in sheet of actual attendees, service invoices, original supporting documents, electronic settlement document, and entrustment agreement (contract) were all complete, and the entire expense was reimbursed in one batch through the same channel.
There was also another office expense in the same batch that included office stationery and computer accessories. The seller provided a goods list together with the invoice. There was no online shopping transaction record, nor did the case involve books and materials, printing and production, or publication contracts and other such materials.

case_ch:
In July 2026, the school processed together remuneration, training organization expenses, and the receipts from a simultaneous trip for a research methodology training program.
The remuneration materials included neither lecture fees nor dissertation review or defense fees, but only one on-site consulting arrangement. The consultant was reported under the category of other personnel. It was conference-related consulting for 3 consecutive days at RMB 350 per day, and there was no case of communication-based consulting charged by project.
The training portion was reported under the standard for division-level and lower personnel. The training lasted 25 days, and the total of lodging, meal, venue and materials expenses, and other expenses was RMB 16,950, including RMB 8,500 for lodging, RMB 3,250 for meals, RMB 1,250 for venue and materials expenses, and RMB 750 for other expenses. During the training period, an instructor from another city was invited, and the total lecture fees were RMB 1,200. The instructor's lodging, meal, and intercity transportation expenses were reimbursed at RMB 340, RMB 130, and RMB 500 respectively, and written approval was also attached for the portion involving an increased standard. However, although the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures" and the list of trainees were both complete, the invoices provided by the training service provider were not submitted.
The itinerary in the same batch was charged to research project funds. The "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" had been completed, signed by the unit head, and affixed with the official seal of the department (the first-level budget unit). Intercity transportation was in first-class train seating. The itinerary was reported as 3 calendar days, with 2 nights in Beijing at RMB 600 per night. The intercity transportation invoice, lodging invoice, and city transportation receipts were all complete. A meal subsidy of RMB 300 was claimed, and city transportation expenses of RMB 180 were reimbursed on an actual basis. There was no cost sharing by an external unit, and there was no self-driving or car rental.

case_ch:
In May 2026, the school simultaneously handled one equipment replacement purchase, one maintenance matter for old equipment, and one set of office expenses for items such as equipment use registers.
Although the purchase materials were accompanied by the "Fudan University Purchased Inventory Confirmation Form (Based on Review Results)," the description of the goods themselves did not match the category of purchased materials. It did not show that this was an ordinary consumable item that failed to meet the fixed asset threshold and did not fall within the management scope of intangible assets. Instead, it looked more like an equipment-type purchase that should not have been handled as purchased materials.
The maintenance matter in the same batch had already first been applied for through the Asset Management Office system, and the "Fudan University Fixed Asset Maintenance Application Form" had been printed out. The form bore the signatures of the relevant personnel and had been affixed with the official seal of the unit, and the original invoice for the maintenance service was also fully attached.
There was also another office expense in the same batch that included office stationery, newspapers, and magazines. The amount did not reach the fixed asset threshold and did not fall within the review scope for externally purchased inventory by the Asset Management Office. The seller provided a goods list together with the invoice. There was no online shopping transaction record, nor did the case involve books and materials, printing and production, or publication contracts and other such materials.

case_ch:
In June 2026, the university held a national academic conference, and after the event submitted together the conference organization expenses, the reception materials from the same period, and a set of remuneration receipts for review.
The conference portion was charged to research project funds. There were about 100 participants, and the conference lasted 2 days. The conference services uniformly arranged lodging and meals. The lodging, meal, and other expenses were reported at RMB 480, RMB 150, and RMB 100 per person per day respectively, for a total of RMB 730 per person per day. The conference notice, agenda, budget form, detailed list of conference expense expenditures, sign-in sheet of actual attendees, service invoices, original supporting documents, electronic settlement document, and entrustment agreement (contract) were all complete, and the entire expense was reimbursed in one batch through the same channel.
The reception materials in the same batch were handled on a one-matter, one-settlement basis. The financial invoices, official letter, "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality List," and "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality Approval Form" were all complete. There were 9 visitors and 3 accompanying staff members from our side. The working meal was reported at RMB 100 per person per day, and one formal banquet at RMB 150 per person per occasion was arranged. However, this set of materials also separately listed lodging arrangements, even though lodging expenses should not be charged under the category of official hospitality.
The remuneration materials included neither dissertation review or defense fees nor expert consulting fees, but only one special lecture. The lecturer was reported under the category for division-level and lower personnel. The lecture fee was actually calculated as 2 class hours at RMB 400 per class hour. This lecture fee was not specifically stated as being reported on an after-tax basis, and it corresponded to no more than a half-day teaching load.

case_ch:
In August 2026, Professor Liu traveled to Singapore to participate in a teaching methodology training program, and after returning to China, submitted together the overseas travel expenses, the training expenditures, and the small office items purchased before departure for review.
For the overseas portion, the expenses were reported under non-research project funds and similar funds. The package included the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" issued by the Exit and Entry Center of the Foreign Affairs Office and the overseas travel expense statement. The expense statement specified the conference registration fee, overseas living expenses, personal incidental allowance, and intercity transportation expenses. The actual mode of transportation was reported under the standard for division-level and lower personnel as economy-class airfare, together with corresponding standard references such as hard-seat train fare and third-class ship cabin. The international airfare was paid by official card, the lodging expense was paid by bank transfer, reimbursement was completed in one batch after returning to China, and the corresponding foreign currency was selected in the Online Reservation Reimbursement system for conversion into RMB.
The training portion was reported under the standard for provincial- or ministerial-level personnel and equivalent personnel. The training lasted 15 days, and the total of lodging, meal, venue and materials expenses, and other expenses was RMB 13,200. This amount also included lecture fees of RMB 1,800 for an instructor from another city, plus the instructor's lodging expense of RMB 500, meal expense of RMB 150, and intercity transportation expense of RMB 400. A separate written approval was attached for the portion involving an increased standard. However, the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures" was not submitted with the package.
In the office expense portion of the same batch, there were both office stationery and computer accessories. The seller provided a goods list together with the invoice. There was no online shopping transaction record, nor did the case involve books and materials, printing and production, or publication contracts and other such materials.

case_ch:
In July 2026, during a family visit, Professor Zhao made a detour to attend a short-term skills training program, and after returning to campus, submitted together the family visit transportation expenses, the training expenditures, and a set of receipts for small office consumables for review.
The family visit portion was reported under the category of an unmarried employee visiting parents. This year's arrangement was one round trip. The actual transportation expense was RMB 700, and the reimbursable amount claimed was also RMB 700. The expenses included only intercity transportation and no local transportation expenses. However, this set of materials did not include the family visit application form.
The training portion was reported under the standard for bureau-level personnel. The training lasted 25 days and did not exceed 30 days. The total of lodging, meal, venue and materials expenses, and other expenses was RMB 16,250, reported respectively as RMB 10,000, RMB 3,750, RMB 1,750, and RMB 750. There were no faculty fees and no lodging, meal, or intercity transportation expenses for instructors from other cities. The "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," the list of trainees and their contact information, and supporting documents such as the detailed expense list provided by the training service provider were all complete.
In the office expense portion of the same batch, there were both office stationery and drinking water. The amount did not reach the fixed asset threshold and did not fall within the review scope for externally purchased inventory by the Asset Management Office. The seller provided a goods list together with the invoice, and there was no online shopping transaction record.

case_ch:
In June 2026, the waterproofing project for a dormitory building reached a new payment stage, and the handling staff member submitted together the project payment materials, the explanation of cost review fees, and the materials related to warranty retention for review.
The engineering portion involved a project exceeding RMB 100,000. The original approval form, the formal seal of the Capital Construction Office, and the Project Review Seal of the Fudan University Renovation Management Office were all in place, and both the head of the Capital Construction Office and the vice president in charge had completed their signatures. This application was for the initial payment, so the original construction contract was attached. The payment amount was handled according to the contract and project progress, with no excess payment and no advance payment ahead of progress.
The cost review fee materials in the same batch already included the "Fudan University Construction Project Cost Review Opinion." The claimed amount was written as the portion to be borne by the university, while the portion to be borne by the construction contractor was listed separately, and a copy of a cost review fee invoice issued in the contractor's name was attached.
For the warranty retention portion, there was already an engineering invoice, and when it was issued, it included the warranty retention in the full amount. The Finance Office had issued the corresponding warranty retention receipt, and this receipt was also attached in the current application. The department head had signed the refund application. However, the warranty period had not yet expired, so the refund conditions had not yet been met.

case_ch:
In May 2026, Professor Sun traveled out for research, and after returning to campus, submitted together his own travel receipts, one set of reception materials, and the office supplies purchased during the trip for review.
The business trip portion was charged to research project funds. The "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" had been completed, signed by the unit head, and affixed with the official seal of the department (the first-level budget unit). Intercity transportation was in first-class train seating. The itinerary was reported as 3 calendar days, with 2 nights in Beijing at RMB 700 per night. The intercity transportation invoice, lodging invoice, and city transportation receipts were all complete. A meal subsidy of RMB 300 was claimed, and city transportation expenses of RMB 180 were reimbursed on an actual basis. There was no cost sharing by an external unit, and there was no self-driving or car rental.
The reception materials in the same batch were organized on a one-matter, one-settlement basis. The financial invoices, "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality List," and "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality Approval Form" had all been prepared. There were 9 visitors and 3 accompanying staff members from our side. The working meal was reported at RMB 100 per person per day, and one formal banquet at RMB 150 per person per occasion was arranged. No lodging expense was claimed separately. However, this set of materials did not include the official letter from the visiting unit.
There was also another office expense in the same batch that included office stationery and office furniture. The amount did not reach the fixed asset threshold and did not fall within the review scope for externally purchased inventory by the Asset Management Office, and the seller provided a goods list together with the invoice.

case_ch: Labor service fees + conference expenses + printing expenses: An academic conference invited two experts to deliver keynote reports, paid labor service fees, paid conference venue rental, and printed expert profile booklets. The first expense was a labor service payment. The doctoral dissertation defense included 5 reviewers, 5 defense committee members, and 1 defense secretary, with the relevant standards at RMB 300, RMB 300, and RMB 150 per person respectively. In addition, a bureau-level person was invited to give a special lecture for a total of 4 class hours, with lecture fees calculated at RMB 900 per class hour. The second expense was for an international conference held in China supported by non-research funds, organized under the Category I conference standard, with about 100 participants and a duration of 2 days. Conference services uniformly arranged lodging at about RMB 300 per person per day, meals at about RMB 200 per person per day, and other expenses at about RMB 250 per person per day, for an overall total of about RMB 750 per person per day. The notice and agenda were complete, the budget form was attached, the expense details were complete, the sign-in sheet specified participating units, and the service contract or agreement was also provided. The third expense was a reimbursement for office stationery and cleaning supplies, submitted either as office consumables or printing-related expenses. The online shopping transaction record was also retained.

case_ch: Training expenses + fixed assets + travel expenses: Two teachers were sent to Xi'an to participate in virtual simulation laboratory training, and during the training a set of teaching-use items was purchased. The first expense was a training expense reimbursement for division-level and lower personnel. The training lasted 35 days, and the total of lodging, meal, venue, materials, transportation, and other expenses was RMB 18,425. This amount included lecturer fees of RMB 1,500, as well as reimbursement for an out-of-town lecturer's lodging of RMB 340, meal expense of RMB 130, and transportation expense of RMB 600. The detailed training expense list, trainee list, and service invoices were all complete. Because the training exceeded 30 days, the excess portion was 5 days. A separate written approval was attached for the portion involving an increased standard. The second expense was a purchase submitted under the category of purchased materials. The purchased item had a physical form. A purchased materials warehousing confirmation form was also submitted. This expenditure was not managed under the fixed asset standard, nor under the intangible asset standard. The third expense was a travel reimbursement under research project funds, handled by an ordinary traveler. In May, the traveler went to Beijing under the standard for bureau-director-level personnel and persons of equivalent rank, chose train travel in first-class train seating, with an itinerary of 3 days and 2 nights of lodging at RMB 600 per night. A meal subsidy of RMB 300 was claimed, and city transportation expenses of RMB 180 were reimbursed on an actual basis. The business trip approval and itinerary materials were attached, the receipts had been signed and sealed by the responsible person, the intercity transportation receipts were complete, and the lodging invoice was provided. Self-driving or car rental was also used on this trip, with fuel and tolls totaling RMB 200. The related travel file explicitly included the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form".

case_ch: Visitor reception expenses + labor service fees + official hospitality expenses: A professor from another university came to campus to give an academic lecture. The university bore the lodging expense, paid lecture remuneration, and arranged a welcome banquet that complied with the official hospitality standard. The first expense was an overseas visitor reception expense. The guest rank was that of guests of other ranks, and lodging was arranged in a four-star hotel. The reimbursement simultaneously included lodging, intercity transportation, routine meals, banquet, local transportation, gifts, and meal allowance subsidies for staff working overtime. The foreign affairs filing had already been completed, and the Fudan University Overseas Expert Work Expense Accounting Report had also been submitted. Routine meal expenses were controlled at RMB 280 per person per day, and the banquet was arranged at RMB 280 per person per occasion. The second expense was a labor service payment. The master's dissertation defense included 2 reviewers, 3 defense committee members, and 1 defense secretary, with the relevant standards at RMB 250, RMB 250, and RMB 100 per person respectively. In addition, an academician was invited to give a special lecture for a total of 4 class hours, with lecture fees calculated at RMB 1,500 per class hour. An on-site consulting arrangement lasting 3 days was also made, with consulting fees paid at RMB 450 per day. The third expense was a domestic official hospitality expense, handled on a one-time event basis, with 9 visitors and 3 accompanying staff members. The official letter from the visiting unit, the hospitality list, and the approval form were all complete. No lodging expense was claimed separately. The working meal was controlled at RMB 100 per person per day, and the formal banquet was arranged at RMB 150 per person per occasion.

case_ch:
In May 2026, while handling the maintenance of a large laboratory incubator, the laboratory submitted together the maintenance expenses, small office supplies, and receipts from a simultaneous trip for review.
Although the maintenance matter had already first been applied for through the Asset Management Office system, the "Fudan University Fixed Asset Maintenance Application Form" was not submitted with the package. Fortunately, the original invoice for the maintenance service was already fully attached.
The office expense in the same batch included only office stationery. The seller provided a goods list together with the invoice. There was no online shopping transaction record, nor did the case involve books and materials, printing and production, or publication contracts and other such materials.
The travel portion was charged to research project funds. The "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" had been completed, signed by the unit head, and affixed with the official seal of the department (the first-level budget unit). The traveler was reported under the category of other personnel, but chose business-class airfare. The itinerary was reported as 3 calendar days, with 2 nights in Beijing at RMB 600 per night. The intercity transportation invoice, lodging invoice, and city transportation receipts were all complete. A meal subsidy of RMB 300 was claimed, and city transportation expenses of RMB 180 were reimbursed on an actual basis. However, the materials did not include any special explanation for taking transportation above the standard.

case_ch: Overseas travel reimbursement + conference expenses + labor service fees: Professor Zhou traveled to the United States to attend an international annual meeting and served as moderator of a breakout session. He paid the conference registration fee and received labor remuneration as a moderator after the event, and reimbursed everything together after returning to China. The first expense was an overseas travel reimbursement for official travel abroad supported by non-research funds. The traveler was division-level or lower personnel, and the air ticket was purchased according to the economy-class standard. The foreign affairs approval and the overseas expense reimbursement form were attached at the time of reimbursement. The expense form listed the registration fee, living allowance, incidental expenses, and transportation expenses. The second expense was a domestic business conference expense supported by research project funds. The conference was organized under the Category I conference standard, with about 100 participants and a duration of 2 days. Conference services uniformly arranged lodging at about RMB 500 per person per day, meals at about RMB 150 per person per day, and other expenses at about RMB 110 per person per day, for an overall total of about RMB 760 per person per day. The notice and agenda were complete, the budget form was attached, the expense details were complete, the sign-in sheet specified participating units, and the service contract or agreement was also provided. The third expense was a labor service payment. Another division-level or lower person was invited to give a special lecture for a total of 2 class hours, with lecture fees calculated at RMB 400 per class hour.

case_ch:
In June 2026, just after the renovation of the chemical laboratory ventilation system was launched, the handling staff member sent a backlog of materials to Finance in one batch: on one side was the initial payment application for the ventilation system project, on the other side were the equipment maintenance receipts dealt with along the way earlier, and there were also a few office supplies temporarily purchased for organizing the construction materials.
The renovation project amount was below RMB 100,000. The original approval form was already attached, and both the formal seal of the Capital Construction Office and the Project Review Seal of the Fudan University Renovation Management Office were complete. This application followed the initial payment process, so the original construction contract was submitted with the package. The payment amount was generally controlled according to the contract and project progress, with no excess payment, except that the initial payment itself exceeded the proportion or amount agreed in the contract.
At the same time, the related equipment maintenance matter had already first been applied for through the Asset Management Office system, and the "Fudan University Fixed Asset Maintenance Application Form" had been printed out, bearing the signatures of the relevant personnel and the official seal of the unit. However, the original invoice for the maintenance service had not yet been fully supplemented. The remaining small purchases mainly consisted of office stationery and computer accessories. The seller provided a goods list together with the invoice. There was no online shopping transaction record, nor did the case involve books and materials, printing and production, or publication contracts and other such materials.

case_ch:
In July 2026, the university organized reimbursement materials in a batch for an activity themed around teaching reform. Around the event, there were both conference organization expenses for a concentrated two-day gathering and a consecutive training program for management personnel, and the materials and routine supplies used on site were also submitted in the same batch.
The two-day concentrated activity was organized for about 100 participants, with conference services uniformly arranging lodging and meals. Lodging expenses were about RMB 480 per person per day, meal expenses about RMB 150 per person per day, and other expenses about RMB 100 per person per day, for a total of about RMB 730 per person per day. The budget form, expense details, sign-in sheet, and service contract or agreement were attached, and the sign-in sheet specified participating units. However, the notice and agenda were not submitted together with the materials.
The training program that followed immediately was reported under the standard for bureau-level personnel and lasted 25 days. The total of lodging, meal, venue, materials, transportation, and other expenses was RMB 16,250. The "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," the trainee list, and the invoices provided by the training service provider were all complete. After the event, some on-site consumables were also claimed, mainly office stationery and drinking water, and the seller provided a sales list together with the invoice.

case_ch:
In May 2026, Professor Wu submitted several consecutively occurring matters together in one batch of materials for review: first the round-trip transportation expenses for privately visiting a spouse, then a research trip to Beijing, and after returning he also participated in a doctoral dissertation defense and arranged a special lecture, so the related receipts and remuneration materials were collected together.
The family visit portion was reported under the category of a married employee making the once-a-year visit to a spouse. The actual expense was RMB 950, and the reimbursable amount claimed was also RMB 950. The expenses included only intercity transportation. Although the actual trip was by airplane, the reimbursed amount was controlled according to the equivalent train fare for the same route. The family visit application form had completed departmental approval and had been affixed with the seal of the personnel department.
For the research trip, the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" was attached, and the responsible person's signature and official seal were complete. The traveler was an ordinary business traveler, but in May traveled to Beijing under the standard for bureau-director-level personnel and persons of equivalent rank. Intercity transportation was in first-class train seating, the itinerary covered 3 calendar days, there were 2 nights in Beijing at RMB 700 per night, a meal subsidy of RMB 300 was claimed, and city transportation expenses of RMB 180 were reimbursed on an actual basis. The intercity transportation receipts and lodging invoice were complete. The defense remuneration materials listed 5 doctoral dissertation reviewers, 5 defense committee members, and 1 defense secretary, with the corresponding standards of RMB 300, RMB 300, and RMB 150 per person respectively. case_ch:
In June 2026, the information platform construction project centrally processed a batch of expenses related to the system launch, including a batch of purchase materials containing application software, remuneration receipts arising from an academic activity held on the same occasion, and transportation and lodging reimbursement for invited external personnel coming to campus.
The purchase portion contained application software, had a unit price exceeding RMB 1,000, was expected to be used for more than 1 year, and belonged to a bulk purchase of similar items. The second copy of the fixed asset reimbursement form had already been attached, and registration had been completed with the asset management department. The second copy of the intangible asset reimbursement form was also attached.
The remuneration materials in the same batch listed 5 doctoral dissertation reviewers, 5 defense committee members, and 1 defense secretary, with the corresponding standards of RMB 300, RMB 300, and RMB 150 per person respectively. In addition, one bureau-level person was invited to give a special lecture for a total of 4 class hours, with lecture fees reported at RMB 900 per class hour. The accompanying transportation and lodging materials were organized using the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form," with the responsible person's signature and official seal complete. The relevant traveler went round trip to Beijing in May under the standard for bureau-director-level personnel and persons of equivalent rank, chose first-class train seating, had an itinerary of 3 calendar days, lodged for 2 nights at RMB 600 per night, claimed a meal subsidy of RMB 300, and reimbursed city transportation expenses of RMB 180 on an actual basis. The intercity transportation receipts and lodging invoice were both provided. However, the actual reimbursement recipient was an invited external person, so the transportation and lodging standard had been separately confirmed by the project leader.

case_ch:
In September 2026, the school received an external institution for cooperation talks. On the same day, it arranged both a working meal and a formal banquet, rented a conference venue at the same time, and after the event also submitted for reimbursement the temporarily purchased reception supplies.
The visitor reception was handled on a one-matter, one-settlement basis, with 9 visitors and 3 accompanying staff members. No lodging expense was claimed separately. The working meal was controlled at RMB 100 per person per day, and the formal banquet was arranged at RMB 150 per person per occasion. However, the reception materials were incomplete, with either the official letter or the "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality Approval Form" missing.
The same batch of materials also included an international conference held in China supported by non-research funds. The conference was organized under the Category I conference standard, with about 100 participants and a duration of 2 days. Conference services uniformly arranged lodging and meals, with lodging at about RMB 300 per person per day, meals at about RMB 200 per person per day, and other expenses at about RMB 250 per person per day, for a total of about RMB 750 per person per day. The notice and agenda, budget form, expense details, sign-in sheet, and service contract or agreement were all complete. The additional purchases made at the event were office stationery and cleaning supplies, which were small expenses from online shopping, and the online shopping transaction detail record was also retained.

case_ch:
In May 2026, after returning to campus from academic exchange in Japan, Professor Zheng processed in one batch the overseas travel expenses, one purchase item for a physical piece of equipment acquired during the trip, and the receipts for a domestic trip connected immediately after returning to China.
For the overseas portion, the case was reported under the category of official overseas travel supported by non-research funds. The traveler took business class under the standard for bureau-level personnel. The foreign affairs approval and the overseas expense reimbursement form were both attached, and the expense form listed the registration fee, living allowance, incidental expenses, and transportation expenses.
The purchase materials in the same batch corresponded to a physical item, and a purchased materials warehousing confirmation form had already been submitted. However, according to the reporting category, this expenditure was not managed under the fixed asset standard, nor under the intangible asset standard. The domestic trip connected after returning to campus was organized using the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form," with the responsible person's signature and official seal complete. The traveler went to Beijing in May under the standard for bureau-director-level personnel and persons of equivalent rank, took first-class train seating, had an itinerary of 3 calendar days, lodged for 2 nights at RMB 600 per night, claimed a meal subsidy of RMB 300, and reimbursed city transportation expenses of RMB 180 on an actual basis. The intercity transportation receipts and lodging invoice were both provided. However, self-driving or car rental was also used on this trip, with fuel and tolls totaling RMB 200.

case_ch:
In August 2026, the Faculty Development Center centrally reimbursed a batch of expenses related to teaching capacity improvement, including the costs of a consecutive training program, remuneration materials arising from an accompanying academic activity, and receipts for small supplies used when printing certificates after the training ended.
The training was reported under the standard for division-level and lower personnel and lasted 15 days. The total of lodging, meal, venue, materials, transportation, and other expenses was RMB 10,740, including lecturer fees of RMB 1,000. The out-of-town lecturer's lodging, meal, and transportation expenses were reimbursed at RMB 340, RMB 130, and RMB 800 respectively. The "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," the trainee list, and the invoices provided by the training service provider were all complete, and a separate written approval was attached for the portion involving an increased standard.
The remuneration materials in the same batch included both a master's dissertation defense and arrangements for lecture and consulting. There were 2 reviewers, 3 defense committee members, and 1 defense secretary, with the corresponding standards of RMB 250, RMB 250, and RMB 100 per person respectively. In addition, one academician was invited to give a special lecture for a total of 4 class hours, with lecture fees reported at RMB 1,500 per class hour, and an on-site consulting arrangement lasting 3 days was also made, with consulting fees paid at RMB 450 per day. The remaining small expenses mainly consisted of office stationery, and the seller provided a sales list together with the invoice.

In June 2026, while advancing a renovation project, the General Affairs Office handling staff member submitted together the project progress payment, the transportation and lodging receipts from an inspection trip, and the office supplies purchased later while organizing the archives.
The engineering portion involved a project below RMB 100,000. The original approval form and related review seals were all in place. This application followed the initial payment process, and the payment amount was controlled according to the contract and project progress, with no overpayment. However, the original construction contract was not submitted with the package.
The inspection trip portion was organized using the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form," with the responsible person's signature and official seal complete. The traveler went to Beijing in May under the standard for bureau-director-level personnel and persons of equivalent rank, chose first-class train seating, had an itinerary of 3 calendar days, lodged for 2 nights at RMB 600 per night, claimed a meal subsidy of RMB 300, and reimbursed city transportation expenses of RMB 180 on an actual basis. The intercity transportation receipts and lodging invoice were both provided. The remaining small purchases were office stationery, newspapers, and magazines, and the seller provided a sales list together with the invoice.

In April 2026, while dealing with a malfunction of an old ultra-low-temperature freezer, the laboratory submitted several related expenses together in one batch of materials: equipment maintenance receipts, a batch of purchase materials that needed to be handled under the asset category, and a remuneration application arising from a short lecture.
The equipment maintenance matter had already first been applied for through the Asset Management Office system, and the "Fudan University Fixed Asset Maintenance Application Form" had been printed out. The form bore the signatures of the relevant personnel and had been affixed with the official seal of the unit, and the original invoice for the maintenance service was also fully attached.
The purchase materials in the same batch corresponded to books, were expected to be used for more than 1 year, and belonged to a bulk purchase of similar items. The second copy of the fixed asset reimbursement form had already been attached, and library accession registration had been completed. The remuneration portion included only one special lecture, with no dissertation review or defense fees and no expert consulting fees. The lecturer was reported under the category for division-level and lower personnel, for a total of 2 class hours, with lecture fees reported at RMB 400 per class hour.

case_ch:
In October 2026, the school undertook a national academic event. In the materials organized centrally after the event, there were both conference organization expenses and receipts for receiving external guests during the conference, as well as the transportation and lodging receipts generated by the person in charge traveling back and forth for the event.
The conference portion was charged to research project funds and was organized under the Category I conference scale, with about 100 participants and a duration of 2 days. Conference services uniformly arranged lodging and meals, with lodging at about RMB 500 per person per day, meals at about RMB 150 per person per day, and other expenses at about RMB 110 per person per day, for a total of about RMB 760 per person per day. The notice and agenda, budget form, detailed list of conference expense expenditures, sign-in sheet, and service contract or agreement were all complete.
The visitor reception during the same period was arranged under the category for guests of other ranks, with lodging in a four-star hotel. The reimbursement simultaneously included lodging, intercity transportation, routine meal expenses, banquet expenses, local transportation, gifts, and meal allowance subsidies for staff working overtime. The foreign affairs filing had already been completed, but the "Fudan University Overseas Expert Work Expense Accounting Report" was not submitted. Routine meal expenses were controlled at RMB 280 per person per day, and the banquet was arranged at RMB 280 per person per occasion. When the person in charge himself traveled for the event, the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" was attached and was additionally signed by another university leader. Intercity transportation was in first-class train seating, the itinerary covered 3 calendar days, there were 2 nights of lodging at RMB 600 per night, a meal subsidy of RMB 300 was claimed, and a lump-sum city transportation allowance of RMB 240 was claimed. The related receipts were complete.

case_ch:
In May 2026, Professor He handled a sequence of personal travel materials in the same reimbursement: first the transportation expenses for returning home to visit parents, then the formalities for overseas travel, and in between some small purchases for visa preparation and routine office supplies were also attached.
The family visit portion was reported under the category of an unmarried employee making the once-a-year visit to parents. The actual expense was RMB 700, and the reimbursable amount claimed was also RMB 700. The expenses included intercity transportation. However, the materials did not include the family visit application form.
The subsequent overseas travel was reported under the category of official overseas travel supported by research funds. The traveler took first-class airfare under the standard for bureau-director-level and above personnel. The foreign affairs approval and the overseas expense reimbursement form were both attached, and the expense form listed the registration fee, living allowance, incidental expenses, and transportation expenses. The small purchases made during the same period mainly consisted of office stationery and drinking water, and the seller provided a sales list together with the invoice.

case_ch:
In November 2026, the school invited external experts to campus for a consecutive training program. After the event, it submitted together the lecture and consulting remuneration, the training organization expenses, and the meal-related reception materials that accompanied the event on the same day for review.
The remuneration materials included no dissertation review or defense fees, but only one relatively long special lecture and on-site consulting. The lecturer was reported under the category for division-level and lower personnel, for a total of 8 class hours, with lecture fees claimed at RMB 500 per class hour. At the same time, an on-site consulting arrangement lasting 2 days was also made, with consulting fees paid at RMB 800 per day.
The training portion was reported under the standard for bureau-level personnel and lasted 40 days. The total of lodging, meal, venue, materials, transportation, and other expenses was RMB 29,250. The "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," the trainee list, and the invoices provided by the training service provider were all complete, and the portion exceeding 30 days was 10 days. On the same day, there was also a domestic official hospitality case, handled on a one-time event basis, with 9 visitors and 3 accompanying staff members. No lodging expense was claimed separately. The working meal was controlled at RMB 100 per person per day, and the formal banquet was arranged at RMB 150 per person per occasion. However, either the official letter or the "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality Approval Form" was missing.

case_ch:
In May 2026, Professor Ma traveled out to participate in a project review, and after returning to campus, submitted together the transportation and lodging receipts from the trip, the printing-related expenses incurred while preparing materials before the review, and the small office purchases used during the review.
The travel portion was reported using the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form," with the responsible person's signature and official seal complete. The traveler was an ordinary business traveler, but went to Beijing in May under the standard for bureau-director-level personnel and persons of equivalent rank, chose first-class train seating, had an itinerary of 3 calendar days, lodged for 2 nights in Beijing at RMB 700 per night, claimed a meal subsidy of RMB 300, and reimbursed city transportation expenses of RMB 180 on an actual basis. The intercity transportation receipts and lodging invoice were both provided.
In another set of receipts related to the preparation for the review, one part consisted of office stationery submitted under the category of office consumables or printing-related expenses, while the other part included both office stationery and office furniture. Both sets of materials were accompanied by a sales list.

case_ch:
In June 2026, when the renovation of the laboratory animal facility reached a payment stage, the handling staff member brought together to Finance the project payment materials, the accompanying cost review documents, and the maintenance receipts for an autoclave in the same area.
The renovation project amount exceeded RMB 100,000. The original approval form and related review seals were all in place, and the payment amount was controlled according to the contract and project progress, with no overpayment. However, because the amount had reached the threshold requiring university leader approval, the materials still lacked the signature of the vice president in charge.
The cost review materials submitted together with the project already included the cost review opinion, and the claimed amount matched the portion to be borne by the university. However, the engineering or equipment invoice was not provided. The equipment maintenance matter had already first been applied for through the Asset Management Office system, and the "Fudan University Fixed Asset Maintenance Application Form" had been printed out, with the relevant signatures and the official seal of the unit, and the original invoice for the maintenance service was complete. The accompanying cost review file explicitly included the "Fudan University Construction Project Cost Review Opinion".

case_ch:
In September 2026, when the school was building a remote exchange platform, it centrally processed a batch of expenses related to an online academic activity, including equipment purchase materials, one conference organization expense, and the transportation and lodging receipts of the personnel traveling back and forth for debugging work.
The purchase portion had already submitted a purchased materials warehousing confirmation form. However, according to the reported category, this physical item was neither managed under the fixed asset standard nor under the intangible asset standard.
The conference portion was charged to research project funds and was organized under the Category I conference scale, with about 100 participants and a duration of 2 days. Conference services uniformly arranged lodging at about RMB 500 per person per day, but meals were not uniformly arranged, and the remaining conference expenses converted to about RMB 650 per person per day. The notice and agenda, budget form, detailed list of conference expense expenditures, sign-in sheet, and service contract or agreement were all complete. The debugging personnel's trip was organized using the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form," with the responsible person's signature and official seal complete. The itinerary covered 3 calendar days, with 2 nights of lodging at RMB 600 per night, a meal subsidy of RMB 300 was claimed, and city transportation expenses of RMB 180 were reimbursed on an actual basis. The intercity transportation receipts and lodging invoice were provided. However, self-driving or car rental was also used on this trip, with fuel and tolls totaling RMB 200.

case_ch:
In August 2026, after returning from an academic activity in Korea, Professor Shen submitted together the overseas travel expenses, the remuneration materials arising from an academic activity held on the same occasion, and the small purchases made while preparing display materials before the event.
For the overseas portion, the case was reported under the category of official overseas travel supported by non-research funds. The traveler was division-level or lower personnel, and the air ticket was purchased according to the economy-class standard. The foreign affairs approval and the overseas expense reimbursement form were both attached, and the expense form listed the registration fee, living allowance, incidental expenses, and transportation expenses.
The remuneration materials in the same batch included a master's dissertation defense as well as a high-level lecture and on-site consulting arrangement. There were 2 reviewers, 3 defense committee members, and 1 defense secretary, with the corresponding standards of RMB 250, RMB 250, and RMB 100 per person respectively. In addition, one academician was invited to give a special lecture for a total of 4 class hours, with lecture fees reported at RMB 1,500 per class hour, and an on-site consulting arrangement lasting 3 days was made, with consulting fees paid at RMB 450 per day. The small purchases made during the same period mainly consisted of office stationery, and the seller provided a sales list together with the invoice.

case_ch:
In July 2026, after returning to campus, Professor Qian submitted several consecutively occurring matters together for review: first a training program lasting 25 days, during which an out-of-town lecturer also came to teach, then the round-trip transportation expenses for visiting parents, and finally the small office supplies purchased for remote study.
The training portion was reported under the standard for division-level and lower personnel. The total of lodging, meal, venue, materials, transportation, and other expenses was RMB 16,950, including lecturer fees of RMB 1,200. The out-of-town lecturer's lodging, meal, and transportation expenses were reimbursed at RMB 340, RMB 130, and RMB 500 respectively. The "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures" and the trainee list were both complete, and a separate written approval was attached for the portion involving an increased standard. However, the invoices provided by the training service provider were not submitted.
The family visit portion was reported under the category of an unmarried employee making the once-a-year visit to parents. The actual expense was RMB 800, and the reimbursable amount claimed was also RMB 800. The expenses included intercity transportation, and the family visit application form had completed departmental approval and had been affixed with the seal of the personnel department. The remaining small purchases mainly consisted of office stationery, newspapers, and magazines, and the seller provided a sales list together with the invoice.

case_ch:
In October 2026, the school received an overseas visiting scholar on campus for exchange. After the event, it submitted together the reception materials formed that day, the overseas expert expense documents during the visit, and the remuneration application for a special lecture.
The on-campus reception on that day was organized under the domestic official hospitality one-matter, one-settlement method, with 9 visitors and 3 accompanying staff members. The official letter from the visiting unit, the "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality List," and the "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality Approval Form" were all complete. No lodging expense was claimed separately. The working meal was controlled at RMB 100 per person per day, and the formal banquet was arranged at RMB 150 per person per occasion.
At the same time, the overseas visitor reception materials had already completed foreign affairs filing, and the "Fudan University Overseas Expert Work Expense Accounting Report" had been submitted. The guests were arranged in a four-star hotel under the category for guests of other ranks, and the reimbursement simultaneously included lodging, intercity transportation, routine meal expenses, banquet expenses, local transportation, gifts, and meal allowance subsidies for staff working overtime. Routine meal expenses were controlled at RMB 320 per person per day, and the banquet was arranged at RMB 280 per person per occasion. There was also remuneration for one special lecture, with the lecturer reported under the category for division-level and lower personnel, for a total of 2 class hours, at RMB 400 per class hour.

In June 2026, while dealing with a malfunction of a flow cytometer, the laboratory brought for reimbursement the maintenance service receipts, the transportation and lodging receipts generated while coordinating the repair, and the small consumables purchased during the maintenance process.

The receipts had the responsible person's signature and official seal complete, and both the intercity transportation receipts and the lodging invoice were provided. However, the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" and the corresponding business trip approval and itinerary materials were not attached. The small purchases made during the same period mainly consisted of office stationery and computer accessories, and the seller provided a sales list together with the invoice.

case_ch:
In October 2026, the school held an annual on-campus academic meeting. After the event, it submitted together several concentrated expenses, including the conference organization expenses, the small supplies purchased for venue setup and material organization, and the remuneration for a high-level special lecture arranged during the annual meeting.
The conference portion was organized under the category of conference activities supported by research funds, at a scale of about 100 participants and a duration of 2 days. Conference services uniformly arranged lodging and meals, with lodging at about RMB 480 per person per day, meals at about RMB 150 per person per day, and other expenses at about RMB 100 per person per day, for a total of about RMB 730 per person per day. The budget form, expense details, sign-in sheet, and service contract or agreement were all complete. However, the notice and agenda were not submitted with the package.
The small purchases related to venue materials mainly consisted of office stationery and computer accessories, and the seller provided a sales list together with the invoice. The remuneration materials included no dissertation review or defense fees and no expert consulting fees, but only one special lecture. The lecturer was reported under the category for bureau-level personnel, for a total of 4 class hours, with lecture fees claimed at RMB 1,200 per class hour.

In June 2026, when handling follow-up matters for a renovation project, the school submitted together the project payment materials, the cost review documents related to the portion to be borne by the university, and the transportation and lodging receipts organized during the same period.
The engineering portion exceeded RMB 100,000 in amount. The original approval form, related review seals, the signature of the head of the Capital Construction Office, and the signature of the vice president in charge were all complete. This application followed the initial payment process, the original construction contract had also been submitted, and the payment amount was controlled according to the contract and project progress, with no overpayment.
The cost review materials in the same batch already included the cost review opinion, the engineering or equipment invoice had been provided, the claimed amount matched the portion to be borne by the university, the portion to be borne by the construction contractor had been listed separately, and a copy of the contractor's invoice had also been provided. Another set of travel materials was organized using the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form," with the responsible person's signature and official seal complete. The itinerary covered 3 calendar days, with 2 nights of lodging at RMB 600 per night, a meal subsidy of RMB 300 was claimed, and city transportation expenses of RMB 180 were reimbursed on an actual basis. However, the intercity transportation receipts were missing, although the lodging invoice had been provided. The accompanying cost review file explicitly included the "Fudan University Construction Project Cost Review Opinion".

Conference services uniformly arranged lodging and meals, with lodging at about RMB 300 per person per day, meals at about RMB 200 per person per day, and other expenses at about RMB 250 per person per day, for a total of about RMB 750 per person per day. In September 2026, when updating analytical equipment, the laboratory submitted together several related expenses: one purchase containing application software, the maintenance receipts for a sensor of old equipment, and a consulting remuneration application generated when an engineer was invited on site for calibration.
The purchase materials clearly included application software, and the second copy of the intangible asset reimbursement form had already been fully attached. The old equipment maintenance matter had already first been applied for through the Asset Management Office system, and the "Fudan University Fixed Asset Maintenance Application Form" had been printed out. The form bore the signatures of the relevant personnel and had been affixed with the official seal of the unit, and the original invoice for the maintenance service was also fully attached.
The remuneration materials in the same batch included no dissertation review or defense fees and no lecture fees, but only an on-site consulting arrangement lasting 3 days, with consulting fees paid at RMB 350 per day.

case_ch:
In August 2026, the school submitted together multiple sets of materials arising from an activity in Australia, including overseas travel expenses, the expenditures of a consecutive training program, and the conference materials from meetings attended during the same period.
For the overseas portion, the case was reported under the category of official overseas travel supported by non-research funds. The traveler was bureau-level personnel, and the air ticket was purchased according to the business-class standard. The foreign affairs approval and the overseas expense reimbursement form were both attached, and the expense form listed the registration fee, living allowance, incidental expenses, and transportation expenses.
The training portion was reported under the standard for division-level and lower personnel and lasted 15 days. The total of lodging, meal, venue, materials, transportation, and other expenses was RMB 10,740, including lecturer fees of RMB 1,000. The out-of-town lecturer's lodging, meal, and transportation expenses were reimbursed at RMB 340, RMB 130, and RMB 800 respectively. The "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," the trainee list, and the invoices provided by the training service provider were all complete, and a separate written approval was attached for the portion involving an increased standard. The conference materials during the same period were organized under the category of conference activities supported by research funds, at a scale of about 100 participants and a duration of 2 days. Conference services uniformly arranged lodging and meals, and the overall materials were complete. However, although foreign guests attended, no foreign affairs approval was seen.

case_ch:
In June 2026, after project acceptance was completed, the handling staff member submitted together several sets of materials arising from experts coming on site to participate in the acceptance, including expert consulting remuneration, the working meal arranged that day, and the related round-trip transportation and lodging receipts.
The remuneration materials included no dissertation review or defense fees and no lecture fees, but only an on-site consulting arrangement lasting 3 days, with consulting fees paid at RMB 350 per day.
The reception on that day was organized under the domestic official hospitality one-matter, one-settlement method, with 9 visitors and 3 accompanying staff members. No lodging expense was claimed separately. The working meal was controlled at RMB 100 per person per day, and the formal banquet was arranged at RMB 150 per person per occasion. However, either the official letter or the "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality Approval Form" was missing. The related travel materials were submitted using the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form," with the responsible person's signature and official seal complete, and both the intercity transportation receipts and the lodging invoice were provided.

case_ch:
In September 2026, while preparing a large international exchange event, the school included in the same reimbursement batch the conference expenses, a set of purchase materials handled under the asset category during the same period, and the small receipts generated while preparing conference materials.
The conference portion was organized under the category of conference activities supported by research funds, at a scale of about 100 participants and a duration of 2 days. Conference services uniformly arranged lodging and meals, with lodging at about RMB 480 per person per day, meals at about RMB 150 per person per day, and other expenses at about RMB 100 per person per day, for a total of about RMB 730 per person per day. The notice and agenda, budget form, expense details, sign-in sheet, and service contract or agreement were all attached.
The purchase materials in the same batch corresponded to books, were expected to be used for more than 1 year, and belonged to a bulk purchase of similar items. The second copy of the fixed asset reimbursement form had already been attached, and accession registration had been completed in the library. Another small purchase related to preparing the materials was submitted under the category of office consumables or printing-related expenses, and the seller provided a sales list together with the invoice. However, there was artificial splitting of invoice amounts.
