case_ch:
In May 2026, the school submitted together several sets of receipts from originally different sources, including one family visit transportation expense, one set of research travel receipts, the conference expenses of an international conference held in China, and two small purchases.
The family visit portion was reported according to the prescribed cycle for a married employee visiting a spouse. The actual expense was RMB 920, and the reimbursable amount claimed was also RMB 920. However, the expenses listed only city transportation. The family visit application form had completed departmental approval and had been affixed with the seal of the personnel department. The subsequent research trip already included the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form," with the responsible person's signature and official seal complete. The itinerary covered 3 calendar days, with 2 nights of lodging at RMB 600 per night, a meal subsidy of RMB 300 was claimed, and city transportation expenses of RMB 180 were reimbursed on an actual basis, but the intercity transportation receipts were missing. The conference materials were organized under the category of an international conference held in China supported by non-research funds, at a scale of about 100 participants and a duration of 2 days. Conference services uniformly arranged lodging and meals, with lodging at about RMB 300 per person per day, meals at about RMB 200 per person per day, and other expenses at about RMB 250 per person per day, for a total of about RMB 750 per person per day. The full set of conference materials was complete.
Among the small purchases, one was office stationery and cleaning supplies, formed through online shopping, and the online shopping transaction detail record was also retained; the other was office stationery and computer accessories, and the seller provided a sales list together with the invoice.

case_ch:
In September 2026, when updating analytical equipment, the laboratory submitted together several related expenses: one purchase containing application software, the maintenance receipts for old equipment, the consulting remuneration generated when an engineer was invited on site for calibration, and two small supplies additionally purchased while recording the process.
The purchase materials clearly included application software, and the second copy of the intangible asset reimbursement form had already been fully attached. The old equipment maintenance matter had already first been applied for through the Asset Management Office system, and the "Fudan University Fixed Asset Maintenance Application Form" had been printed out. The form bore the signatures of the relevant personnel and had been affixed with the official seal of the unit, and the original invoice for the maintenance service was also fully attached. The remuneration materials included no dissertation review or defense fees and no lecture fees, but only an on-site consulting arrangement lasting 3 days, with consulting fees paid at RMB 350 per day.
Among the small purchases, one was office stationery and computer accessories, and the other was office stationery. Both sets of materials were accompanied by a sales list.

case_ch:
In August 2026, the school submitted together multiple sets of materials arising from an activity in Australia, including overseas travel expenses, the expenditures of a consecutive training program, the conference materials from meetings attended during the same period, and the small purchases made while organizing materials after the event.
The overseas portion was reported under the category of official overseas travel supported by non-research funds. The traveler was bureau-level personnel, and the air ticket was purchased according to the business-class standard. The foreign affairs approval and the overseas expense reimbursement form were both attached, and the expense form listed the registration fee, living allowance, incidental expenses, and transportation expenses. The training portion was reported under the standard for division-level and lower personnel and lasted 15 days. The total of lodging, meal, venue, materials, transportation, and other expenses was RMB 10,740, including lecturer fees of RMB 1,000, together with reimbursement for an out-of-town lecturer's lodging of RMB 340, meal expenses of RMB 130, and transportation of RMB 800. The "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," the trainee list, and the invoices provided by the training service provider were all complete, and a separate written approval was attached for the portion involving an increased standard. The conference materials during the same period were organized under the category of conference activities supported by research funds, at a scale of about 100 participants and a duration of 2 days. Conference services uniformly arranged lodging and meals, and the overall materials were complete. However, although foreign guests attended, no foreign affairs approval was seen.
Among the small purchases, one was office stationery, and the other was office stationery, newspapers, and magazines. Both sets of materials were accompanied by a sales list.

case_ch:
In September 2026, the laboratory submitted together several materials related to equipment maintenance, including one physical purchase, one equipment maintenance case, one set of research travel receipts, one small purchase, and one set of visitor reception documents.
The purchase materials had already submitted a purchased materials warehousing confirmation form. However, according to the reported category, this physical item was neither managed under the fixed asset standard nor under the intangible asset standard. The equipment maintenance matter had already been applied for through the Asset Management Office system, but the "Fudan University Fixed Asset Maintenance Application Form" was not submitted with the package, although the original invoice for the maintenance service was complete. The related travel materials were organized using the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form," with the responsible person's signature and official seal complete, and both the intercity transportation receipts and the lodging invoice were provided. The small purchase was office stationery, newspapers, and magazines, and the seller provided a sales list together with the invoice.
The visitor reception portion was arranged under the category for guests of other ranks, with lodging in a four-star hotel. The reimbursement simultaneously included lodging, intercity transportation, routine meal expenses, banquet expenses, local transportation, gifts, and meal allowance subsidies for staff working overtime. The foreign affairs filing had already been completed, but the "Fudan University Overseas Expert Work Expense Accounting Report" was not submitted. Routine meal expenses were controlled at RMB 280 per person per day, and the banquet was arranged at RMB 280 per person per occasion.

case_ch:
In October 2026, after receiving a visit from peer institutions and holding an exchange activity, the school submitted together the reception materials, the conference expenses, the related travel receipts, and two small purchases.
The reception portion was organized under the domestic official hospitality one-matter, one-settlement method, with 9 visitors and 3 accompanying staff members. No lodging expense was claimed separately. The working meal was controlled at RMB 100 per person per day, and the formal banquet was arranged at RMB 150 per person per occasion. However, either the official letter or the "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality Approval Form" was missing. The conference portion was organized under the category of conference activities supported by research funds, at a scale of about 100 participants and a duration of 2 days. Conference services uniformly arranged lodging and meals, with lodging at about RMB 480 per person per day, meals at about RMB 150 per person per day, and other expenses at about RMB 100 per person per day, for a total of about RMB 730 per person per day. The full set of conference materials was complete. The travel materials were reported under the standard for other personnel, but business-class airfare was chosen, with the related receipts complete.
Among the small purchases, one was office stationery and involved artificial splitting of invoice amounts, and the other was office stationery and computer accessories. Both sets of materials were accompanied by a sales list. The related travel file explicitly included the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form".

case_ch:
In June 2026, during the advancement of the university history museum renovation, the handling staff member submitted together the project payment materials, two small purchases, the transportation and lodging receipts from the person in charge's own trip, and the accompanying cost review documents.
The engineering portion involved a project below RMB 100,000. The original approval form and related review seals were all complete, and the payment amount was controlled according to the contract and project progress, with no overpayment. Among the small purchases, one was office stationery, newspapers, and magazines, and the other was office stationery, but the latter involved artificial splitting of invoice amounts. Both sets of materials were accompanied by a sales list. The person in charge's own trip had already been countersigned by another university leader, the related receipts were complete, and a lump-sum city transportation allowance of RMB 240 was claimed.
In the cost review materials of the same batch, the engineering or equipment invoice had been provided, the cost review opinion had already been attached, the claimed amount matched the portion to be borne by the university, the portion to be borne by the construction contractor had been listed separately, and a copy of the contractor's invoice had also been provided. The related travel file explicitly included the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form". The accompanying cost review file explicitly included the "Fudan University Construction Project Cost Review Opinion".

case_ch:
In July 2026, the school arranged teachers to participate in a round of online course production training. After returning to campus, it submitted together the training expenses, the transportation and lodging receipts from a simultaneous trip, one set of purchase materials containing application software, and two small purchases.
The training was reported under the standard for bureau-director-level and above personnel and lasted 20 days. The total of lodging, meal, venue, materials, transportation, and other expenses was RMB 18,720, including lecturer fees of RMB 2,500, together with reimbursement for an out-of-town lecturer's lodging of RMB 600, meal expenses of RMB 200, and transportation of RMB 900. The "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," the trainee list, and the invoices provided by the training service provider were all complete. In the related travel materials, both the intercity transportation receipts and the lodging invoice were provided, and the responsible person's signature and official seal were also complete. However, the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" and the corresponding business trip approval and itinerary materials were not attached. The purchase materials clearly contained application software, and the second copy of the intangible asset reimbursement form had already been fully attached.
Among the small purchases, one was office stationery and drinking water, and the other was office stationery. Both sets of materials were accompanied by a sales list.

case_ch:
In September 2026, the school centrally submitted multiple sets of materials for a large international exchange event, including the conference organization expenses, high-level visitor reception, purchase receipts containing application software, one set of research travel receipts, and one small purchase.
The conference portion was organized under the category of conference activities supported by research funds, at a scale of about 100 participants and a duration of 2 days. Conference services uniformly arranged lodging and meals, with lodging at about RMB 480 per person per day, meals at about RMB 150 per person per day, and other expenses at about RMB 100 per person per day, for a total of about RMB 730 per person per day. However, the notice and agenda were not submitted with the package. The visitor reception portion was arranged under the standard for ministerial- or vice-ministerial-level guests, with lodging in a five-star hotel. The reimbursement simultaneously included lodging, intercity transportation, routine meal expenses, banquet expenses, local transportation, gifts, and meal allowance subsidies for staff working overtime. The foreign affairs filing had already been completed, and the "Fudan University Overseas Expert Work Expense Accounting Report" had also been submitted. Routine meal expenses were controlled at RMB 500 per person per day, the banquet was arranged at RMB 400 per person per occasion, and the visiting delegation was led by vice-ministerial-level personnel or above. The purchase materials clearly contained application software, had a unit price exceeding RMB 1,000, were expected to be used for more than 1 year, and belonged to a bulk purchase of similar items. The second copy of the fixed asset reimbursement form and the second copy of the intangible asset reimbursement form had both been fully attached, and registration had been completed with the asset management department.
In another set of research travel materials, the intercity transportation receipts were missing although the lodging invoice had been provided. The small purchase was office stationery and office furniture, and the seller provided a sales list together with the invoice. The related travel file explicitly included the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form".

case_ch:
In May 2026, Professor Wu submitted together the materials from a consecutive itinerary: first returning home under the arrangement for visiting a spouse, then connecting with a research trip and participating in a relatively long conference, while also attaching the small supplies purchased during the conference and one set of physical purchase materials.
The family visit portion was reported under the category of a married employee making the once-a-year visit to a spouse. The actual expense was RMB 950, and the reimbursable amount claimed was also RMB 950. The expenses included intercity transportation. Although the actual trip was by airplane, the reimbursed amount was controlled according to the equivalent train fare for the same route. The family visit application form had completed departmental approval and had been affixed with the seal of the personnel department. The research trip already included the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form," with the responsible person's signature and official seal complete. The itinerary covered 3 calendar days, with 2 nights of lodging at RMB 700 per night, a meal subsidy of RMB 300 was claimed, and city transportation expenses of RMB 180 were reimbursed on an actual basis. The intercity transportation receipts and lodging invoice were both provided. The conference portion was organized under the category of conference activities supported by research funds, at a scale of about 100 participants and a duration of 3 days. Conference services uniformly arranged lodging and meals, with lodging at about RMB 480 per person per day, meals at about RMB 150 per person per day, and other expenses at about RMB 100 per person per day, for a total of about RMB 730 per person per day. The full set of conference materials was complete.
The small purchase was office stationery and office furniture, and the seller provided a sales list together with the invoice. Another set of purchase materials corresponded to physical items, and a purchased materials warehousing confirmation form had already been submitted. However, according to the reported category, this expenditure was neither managed under the fixed asset standard nor under the intangible asset standard.

case_ch:
In June 2026, before and after an overseas visit, Professor Lin placed several related sets of materials into the same batch for review, including one overseas travel expense, one engineering payment application, two small purchases, and one set of transportation and lodging receipts from a domestic inspection trip.
The overseas portion was reported under the category of official overseas travel supported by non-research funds. The traveler was division-level or lower personnel, and the air ticket was purchased according to the economy-class standard. The foreign affairs approval and the overseas expense reimbursement form were both attached, and the expense form listed the registration fee, living allowance, incidental expenses, and transportation expenses. The engineering portion exceeded RMB 100,000 in amount. The original approval form and related review seals were all complete, and the payment amount was controlled according to the contract and project progress, with no overpayment. However, because the amount had reached the threshold requiring university leader approval, the materials still lacked the signature of the vice president in charge.
Among the small purchases, one was office stationery and office furniture, and the other was office stationery and cleaning supplies, with the latter formed through online shopping and accompanied by the online shopping transaction detail record. The domestic inspection materials already included the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form," with the responsible person's signature and official seal complete, and the related receipts were all provided. However, self-driving or car rental was also used on this trip, with fuel and tolls totaling RMB 200.
